speaker: Unknown
en: A lot of product teams, a lot of founders built something.
zh: 很多产品团队、很多创始人构建了一些东西。

speaker: Unknown
en: It starts to show some success and then all of a sudden it just stops growing.
zh: 它开始显示出一些成功，然后突然它就停止增长了。

speaker: Unknown
en: There's a series of questions that I asked to diagnose why is growth slowing.
zh: 我会问一系列问题来诊断为什么增长在放缓。

speaker: Unknown
en: The first question is, are customers leaving?
zh: 第一个问题是，客户正在离开吗？

speaker: Unknown
en: Think about the gauntlet they went through to get to the product.
zh: 想想他们为了接触产品所经历的磨难。

speaker: Unknown
en: How did they even find out about me?
zh: 他们到底是怎么发现我的？

speaker: Unknown
en: That was hard already and improbable.
zh: 那已经很难且不太可能了。

speaker: Unknown
en: They didn't just bounce off the homepage, which is again, improbable.
zh: 他们没有从首页直接流失，这再次说明了不太可能。

speaker: Unknown
en: And they got to the pricing page.
zh: 而且他们来到了定价页面。

speaker: Unknown
en: That didn't scare them off.
zh: 那并没有把他们吓跑。

speaker: Unknown
en: They actually had the budget and bought the stupid thing.
zh: 他们实际上有预算并且买了这破玩意儿。

speaker: Unknown
en: And after all of that, which clearly means they wanted it to work, they're like, "No, bye."
zh: 经过所有这一切，这清楚地表明他们希望它能成功，他们却说，“不，再见。”

speaker: Unknown
en: What?
zh: 什么？

speaker: Unknown
en: Just on an emotional level, you got to go, "Wait a minute, that's terrible."
zh: 仅仅在情绪层面上，你不得不去想，“等等，这太糟糕了。”

speaker: Unknown
en: Step two is pricing, positioning.
zh: 第二步是定价、定位。

speaker: Unknown
en: Your prices are way too low because you just guessed and you haven't changed them.
zh: 你的价格太低了，因为你只是瞎猜的，而且你没有改变它们。

speaker: Unknown
en: What often happens is you raise prices and signups don't change.
zh: 经常发生的是你提高了价格，注册量却没有变化。

speaker: Unknown
en: Just think about a company with a thousand employees and 400 million in revenue or whatever.
zh: 想想一家有一千名员工和四亿收入之类的公司。

speaker: Unknown
en: If they see a product that's $2 a month or even $100 a month, thought is like, that can't be good enough.
zh: 如果他们看到一个每月 2 美元甚至每月 100 美元的产品，想法会像，这肯定不够好。

speaker: Unknown
en: We positioned this conversation as how to deal with stalled growth, but it's actually just as useful for how do I grow more?
zh: 我们把这次对话定位为如何处理停滞的增长，但这对于“我要如何增长得更多”同样有用。

speaker: Unknown
en: Do you know right now which channels are saturated and which aren't?
zh: 你现在知道哪些渠道是饱和的，哪些不是吗？

speaker: Unknown
en: You can't just rely on marketing forever.
zh: 你不能永远只靠营销。

speaker: Unknown
en: Just adding one little feature and then hoping we can flog AdWords is not going to work.
zh: 仅仅增加一个小功能然后希望我们能以此利用 AdWords 是行不通的。

speaker: Lenny
en: What comes next?
zh: 接下来是什么？

speaker: Unknown
en: The last question is, do you need to grow?
zh: 最后一个问题是，你需要增长吗？

speaker: Unknown
en: We all have heard the phrase, "If you're not growing, you're dying."
zh: 我们都听说过这句话，“如果你没有在增长，你就在消亡。”

speaker: Unknown
en: Is that true or is that the kind of thing that investors use to make founders try to grow even when they shouldn't?
zh: 这是真的，还是那种投资者用来让创始人在不应该增长的时候也尝试增长的话术？

speaker: Unknown
en: Today, my guest is Jason Cohen.
zh: 今天，我的嘉宾是 Jason Cohen。

speaker: Unknown
en: Jason is a four-time founder, including two unicorns, one being WP Engine.
zh: Jason 是一位四次创始人，包括两家独角兽，其中一家是 WP Engine。

speaker: Unknown
en: He's not just an incredible builder and entrepreneur.
zh: 他不仅是一位不可思议的构建者和企业家。

speaker: Unknown
en: He's also an incredible writer and share of product wisdom.
zh: 他还是一位不可思议的作家和产品智慧的分享者。

speaker: Unknown
en: He's been sharing his advice online for over 20 years now.
zh: 20 多年来，他一直在网上分享他的建议。

speaker: Unknown
en: I've been a huge fan of Jason's from afar for so long and it was such a treat to have him on the podcast.
zh: 长久以来我一直从远处关注着 Jason，能邀请他上播客真是件乐事。

speaker: Unknown
en: There are a million things we could have talked about and I'm definitely going to have him back.
zh: 我们本可以谈论一百万件事情，我肯定会再邀请他。

speaker: Unknown
en: In this conversation, we spent the entire time talking about his very actionable and very helpful framework for what to do when your product's growth stalls.
zh: 在这次对话中，我们花了一整段时间讨论他的非常可执行且非常有用的框架，用于当你的产品增长停滞时该怎么做。

speaker: Unknown
en: I found his way of looking at the problem incredibly practical and real and actionable.
zh: 我发现他看待问题的方式非常实用、真实且可执行。

speaker: Unknown
en: And if you're looking for ideas for how to rekindle your product's growth or just accelerate the growth of your product, you're going to walk away from this conversation with your mind buzzing.
zh: 如果你正在寻找如何重新点燃你的产品增长，或者仅仅是加速你的产品增长的主意，你会带着满脑子兴奋的想法从这次对话中离开。

speaker: Unknown
en: Also, I'll add that after 20 years of blogging online, Jason is about to publish his very first real book.
zh: 另外，我要补充的是，经过 20 年的在线博客写作，Jason 即将出版他的第一本真正的书。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's called Hidden Multipliers.
zh: 它叫 Hidden Multipliers。

speaker: Unknown
en: You can now pre-order it online at hiddenmultipliers.com.
zh: 你现在可以在 hiddenmultipliers.com 上在线预订。

speaker: Unknown
en: I am going to grab a bunch.
zh: 我要去抢一堆。

speaker: Unknown
en: I bet after listening to this conversation, you will too.
zh: 我敢打赌听了这次对话后，你也会的。

speaker: Unknown
en: If you enjoy this podcast, don't forget to subscribe and follow it in your favorite podcasting app or YouTube.
zh: 如果你喜欢这个播客，别忘了在你最喜欢的播客应用或 YouTube 上订阅和关注它。

speaker: Unknown
en: And if you become an insider subscriber of my newsletter, you get a year free of over 20 incredible products, including a year free of Lovable, Replit, Bolt, Gamma, N8N, Linear, Devin, PostHog, Superhuman, Descript, Wispr Flow, Perplexity, Warp, Granola, Magic Pattern, Raycast, ChatPRD, Mobbin, and Stripe Atlas.
zh: 如果你成为我通讯的内部订阅者，你将免费获得一年超过 20 款不可思议的产品，包括免费一年 Lovable、Replit、Bolt、Gamma、N8N、Linear、Devin、PostHog、Superhuman、Descript、Wispr Flow、Perplexity、Warp、Granola、Magic Pattern、Raycast、ChatPRD、Mobbin 和 Stripe Atlas。

speaker: Unknown
en: Head it over to lennysnewsletter.com and click Product Pass.
zh: 前往 lennysnewsletter.com 并点击 Product Pass。

speaker: Unknown
en: With that, I bring you Jason Cohen after a short word from our sponsors.
zh: 话不多说，在简短的赞助商致辞之后，我为您请来 Jason Cohen。

speaker: Unknown
en: This episode is brought to you by 10Web, the company that pioneered AI website building before ChatGPT.
zh: 本集由 10Web 为您带来，那家在 ChatGPT 之前开创了 AI 网站构建的公司。

speaker: Unknown
en: In the last three years, over two million websites have been generated with 10Web's vibe coding platform.
zh: 在过去三年里，超过两百万个网站是通过 10Web 的 vibe coding 平台生成的。

speaker: Unknown
en: 10Web's vibe coding platform is a powerful way to build websites.
zh: 10Web 的 vibe coding 平台是构建网站的一种强大方式。

speaker: Unknown
en: Think of it as Lovable for WordPress, front end and backend.
zh: 把它想象成 WordPress 版的 Lovable，前端和后端。

speaker: Unknown
en: Users can build any website at any complexity, e-commerce, portfolios, information websites, blogs, and it comes with the WordPress Admin Panel and thousands of ready to use plugins.
zh: 用户可以构建任何复杂程度的网站，电子商务网站、作品集、信息网站、博客，并且它带有 WordPress 管理面板和数千个即用型插件。

speaker: Unknown
en: 10Web also offers website generation as an API as a service for SaaS companies, marketplaces, hosting providers, MSPs, and agencies.
zh: 10Web 还提供网站生成作为 API 服务，面向 SaaS 公司、市场平台、托管服务提供商、MSP 和 agencies。

speaker: Unknown
en: SaaS companies can embed it via API so that users can launch AI-generated sites directly inside of their platform connected to their own data.
zh: SaaS 公司可以通过 API 嵌入它，以便用户可以直接在其平台内部启动 AI 生成的网站，并连接到他们自己的数据。

speaker: Unknown
en: Agencies and MSPs can get a white label dashboard to manage clients and resell under their brand.
zh: Agencies 和 MSP 可以获得白标仪表板来管理客户并以自己的品牌转售。

speaker: Unknown
en: Hosting providers can self-host the API builder on their own infrastructure.
zh: 托管服务提供商可以在自己的基础设施上自托管该 API 构建器。

speaker: Unknown
en: Check it out at 10web.io/lenny and use code Lenny for exclusive free credits and 30% off API or white-labeled solutions.
zh: 请访问 10web.io/lenny 查看详情，并使用代码 Lenny 获得专属免费额度以及 API 或白标解决方案 30% 的折扣。

speaker: Unknown
en: That's the number 10, W-E-B, .io/lenny.
zh: 那是数字 10，W-E-B，.io/lenny。

speaker: Unknown
en: Vibe coding platform as an API.
zh: Vibe coding 平台即 API。

speaker: Unknown
en: This episode is brought to you by Strella, the customer research platform built for the AI era.
zh: 本集由 Strella 为您带来，它是为 AI 时代构建的客户研究平台。

speaker: Unknown
en: Here's the truth about user research.
zh: 关于用户研究有一个真相。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's never been more important or more painful.
zh: 它从未变得如此重要，也从未如此痛苦。

speaker: Unknown
en: Teams want to understand why customers do what they do, but recruiting users running interviews and analyzing insights takes weeks.
zh: 团队想要了解客户为什么做他们所做的事，但招募用户、运行面试和分析洞察需要数周时间。

speaker: Unknown
en: By the time the results are in, the moment to act has passed.
zh: 等到结果出来时，行动的时机已经过去了。

speaker: Unknown
en: Strella changes that.
zh: Strella 改变了这一点。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's the first platform that uses AI to run and analyze in-depth interviews automatically, bringing fast and continuous user research to every team.
zh: 它是第一个使用 AI 自动运行和分析深度面试的平台，为每个团队带来快速且连续的用户研究。

speaker: Unknown
en: Strella's AI moderator asks real follow-up questions, probing deeper when answers are vague, and services patterns across hundreds of conversations all in a few hours, not weeks.
zh: Strella 的 AI 主持人会询问真正的追问问题，在回答模糊时深入探究，并在数百次对话中发现模式，这一切只需几个小时，而不是几周。

speaker: Unknown
en: Product, design, and research teams at companies like Amazon and Duolingo are already using Strella for Figma prototype testing, concept validation, and customer journey research, getting insights overnight instead of waiting for the next sprint.
zh: Amazon 和 Duolingo 等公司的产品、设计和研究团队已经在使用 Strella 进行 Figma 原型测试、概念验证和客户旅程研究，过夜就能获得洞察，而不是等待下一个冲刺周期。

speaker: Unknown
en: If your team wants to understand customers at the speed you ship products, try Strella.
zh: 如果您的团队希望以发布产品的速度了解客户，请尝试 Strella。

speaker: Unknown
en: Run your next study at strella.io/lenny.
zh: 在 strella.io/lenny 运行您的下一个研究。

speaker: Unknown
en: That's S-T-R-E-L-L-A, .io/lenny.
zh: 那是 S-T-R-E-L-L-A，.io/lenny。

speaker: Unknown
en: Jason, thank you so much for being here and welcome to the podcast.
zh: Jason，非常感谢你来到这里，欢迎收听播客。

speaker: Unknown
en: Thank you.
zh: 谢谢。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's an honor to be here.
zh: 很荣幸来到这里。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's an honor to have you here.
zh: 很荣幸你能来到这里。

speaker: Unknown
en: I have wanted to get you on this podcast for so long.
zh: 我想让你上这播客想了很久了。

speaker: Unknown
en: You are both an incredible builder and a founder, and you are such a great communicator.
zh: 你既是一个不可思议的构建者也是一个创始人，而且你是一个非常棒的沟通者。

speaker: Unknown
en: You have been writing at asmartbear.com, which I want to get the backstory on for so long.
zh: 你一直 asmartbear.com 上写作，我想了解它的背景故事很久了。

speaker: Unknown
en: How long have you been writing there, by the way?
zh: 顺便问一下，你在那里写多久了？

speaker: Unknown
en: Almost 20 years.
zh: 快 20 年了。

speaker: Unknown
en: I started when blogging was cool.
zh: 我开始写的时候博客还很酷。

speaker: Unknown
en: And I'm still waiting for blogging to come back and be cool again, but it's not yet.
zh: 我还在等博客回来并再次变酷，但这还没发生。

speaker: Lenny
en: I think it is cool.
zh: 我觉得它很酷。

speaker: Unknown
en: It is?
zh: 是吗？

speaker: Unknown
en: Okay.
zh: 好的。

speaker: Unknown
en: Newsletters are cool now.
zh: 现在时事通讯很酷。

speaker: Unknown
en: Yeah.
zh: 是的。

speaker: Unknown
en: Newsletters are cool.
zh: 时事通讯很酷。

speaker: Unknown
en: Okay.
zh: 好的。

speaker: Lenny
en: I don't know if you saw Twitter now is encouraging long-form writing. There's this articles feature, so I think it's cool. I think you've survived the trough.
zh: 不知道你有没有看到，Twitter 现在在鼓励长文写作。有个文章功能，所以我觉得这很酷。我觉得你已经挺过了低谷期。

speaker: Unknown
en: Yes.
zh: 是的。

speaker: Unknown
en: Okay.
zh: 好的。

speaker: Unknown
en: I was also out talking to Gemini trying to figure out how many posts you've written.
zh: 我刚才也问了 Gemini，想看看你写过多少篇文章。

speaker: Unknown
en: I was like, "Count the number of blog posts on a smartbear.com."
zh: 我说，“数一下 a smartbear.com 上的博客文章数量。”

speaker: Unknown
en: Do you have a sense of how many things you've written on there?
zh: 你知道你在那里写了多少东西吗？

speaker: Unknown
en: Yeah, it's not that many.
zh: 是的，不算多。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's something like maybe a hundred and... Well, I would say between 150 and 200 that I'm proud of and probably about 300, 350.
zh: 大概大概一百……嗯，我想说在 150 到 200 篇之间是我感到自豪的，大概可能有 300、350 篇。

speaker: Unknown
en: And that's it over about 18 years.
zh: 这就是在 18 年里的全部了。

speaker: Unknown
en: And that's because I only write in-depth.
zh: 这是因为我只写深度内容。

speaker: Unknown
en: Some are long, not all are long, but none are short, I guess.
zh: 有些很长，不是都长，但都不短，我想。

speaker: Unknown
en: And I've always had a rule, even though you're supposed to write really regularly and not just for algorithms, but they used to say, again, back in the aughts where I started, "Oh yeah, it needs to be really regular so people know when to expect your thing and they plug it into their day and all this."
zh: 我一直有一个规则，尽管你应该非常规律地写作，而不仅仅是为了算法，但他们过去常说，回到我刚开始的那个年代，也就是 2000 年代，“噢是的，它需要非常规律，这样人们就知道什么时候期待你的东西，并把它插入他们的一天中，诸如此类。”

speaker: Unknown
en: So it's always been true that you should be regular.
zh: 所以你应该保持规律总是对的。

speaker: Unknown
en: And I never was because my attitude was always, I will only put out stuff if it's the best that I can do.
zh: 而我从来不是这样，因为我的态度一直是，如果这是我能做到的最好的，我才会发布内容。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's up to the reader to decide if it's good or useful.
zh: 它是好还是有用的，这取决于读者。

speaker: Unknown
en: And so if I don't have that, I'm just not going to publish.
zh: 所以如果我没有那样的东西，我就只是不发布。

speaker: Unknown
en: That's the way it is.
zh: 就是这样。

speaker: Unknown
en: And so there's years where I've published once or twice only the whole year.
zh: 所以有些年份我一整年只发布了一两次。

speaker: Unknown
en: Maybe I was busy or didn't have the energy.
zh: 也许我很忙或者没有精力。

speaker: Unknown
en: Other years were, yeah, I posted 40 times or something, but even then, it's only that, because I can't do something of that magnitude.
zh: 其他年份是，是的，我发布了 40 次或什么的，但即便那样，也就只有那样，因为我无法做那种量级的事情。

speaker: Unknown
en: And also found unicorns, which I did during that same time and run them.
zh: 并且还发现了独角兽公司，我在那段时间同样这么做了并经营它们。

speaker: Unknown
en: I can't do that all at the same time and produce a lot.
zh: 我无法同时做所有这些事情并产出大量内容。

speaker: Unknown
en: So it's fewer and hopefully better, but that's in the eye of the reader, of course.
zh: 所以是更少，并且希望更好，但这当然在读者的眼中。

speaker: Unknown
en: I like what you say.
zh: 我喜欢你说的话。

speaker: Unknown
en: I've done 300, not too many.
zh: 我做了 300 个，不算太多。

speaker: Unknown
en: Well, not for 18 years.
zh: 嗯，不是在 18 年里。

speaker: Unknown
en: Over that time, you expect-
zh: 在那段时间里，你会预期——

speaker: Unknown
en: Well, I think this actually, this is where I was going to go, but I think this is a really important lesson I've also learned.
zh: 嗯，我想其实，这正是我想去的地方，但我认为这是我学到的非常重要的一个教训。

speaker: Unknown
en: I always used to tell people the key to being successful writing stuff online and just content in general is quality and consistency, but I've just more and more realized quality is actually the only thing that matters and the consistency doesn't matter.
zh: 我过去总是告诉人们，在网上写东西成功以及仅仅是内容成功的关键是质量和一致性，但我只是越来越意识到质量实际上是唯一重要的事情，而一致性并不重要。

speaker: Unknown
en: So the only difference is the more rarely write, the more awesome it has to be.
zh: 所以唯一的区别是，你写得越少，它就必须越精彩。

speaker: Unknown
en: It is a lot of pressure.
zh: 这是一个很大的压力。

speaker: Unknown
en: I feel that.
zh: 我感觉到了。

speaker: Unknown
en: And then I tell myself, that will just prevent you from writing anything and that's not good.
zh: 然后我告诉自己，那只会让你无法写出任何东西，这不好。

speaker: Unknown
en: So yeah, you tend to want everything you make to be the best thing you've ever made.
zh: 所以是的，你往往希望你制作的每件事都是你做过的最好的事。

speaker: Unknown
en: And on the one hand, I want to hold onto that because it's motivation to be good and not to let the bar slip.
zh: 一方面，我想坚持这一点，因为它是保持良好和不降低标准的动力。

speaker: Unknown
en: On the other hand, you can go into paralysis, which is obviously bad.
zh: 另一方面，你可能会陷入瘫痪，这显然是不好的。

speaker: Unknown
en: So yeah, I still struggle with that, but I think that is the tension.
zh: 所以是的，我仍在为此挣扎，但我认为这就是这种张力。

speaker: Unknown
en: Okay.
zh: 好的。

speaker: Unknown
en: So with 300-ish posts, 200 you're proud of, there are so many directions we can go.
zh: 所以有了大约 300 篇文章，其中 200 篇你很自豪，我们可以走的方向太多了。

speaker: Unknown
en: There's a few that I've picked that I want to spend most of our time on.
zh: 我选了几个我想花大部分时间讨论的。

speaker: Unknown
en: The first is you have a really pragmatic way of approaching growth stalling.
zh: 第一个是，你有一种非常务实的方法来处理增长停滞。

speaker: Unknown
en: And the reason I want to spend time here is because a lot of product teams, a lot of founders build something, it starts to show some success, it's going, it's growing, and then all of a sudden it just stops growing.
zh: 我想在这里花时间的原因是，很多产品团队、很多创始人构建了某种东西，它开始显示出一些成功，它在运转，它在增长，然后突然它就停止增长了。

speaker: Unknown
en: And I think that's one of the most painful things to go through.
zh: 我认为这是最痛苦的经历之一。

speaker: Unknown
en: And I've never come across a way to think about how do I solve this?
zh: 我从来没有遇到过一种思考方法来解决“我该如何解决这个问题？”

speaker: Unknown
en: Because I think a lot of people are just like, "Okay, I guess that is not working.
zh: 因为我认为很多人就像，“好吧，我想这是行不通的。

speaker: Unknown
en: Let's move on to something else."
zh: 让我们继续做别的事情吧。”

speaker: Unknown
en: You have a very specific way of approaching this problem.
zh: 你有一种非常具体的方法来处理这个问题。

speaker: Unknown
en: And I want to read actually a quote from Will Smith, and this is something that has stuck with me ever since I read it because it's so true.
zh: 我想读一句威尔·史密斯的话，自从我读到它以来，这句话一直让我念念不忘，因为它是如此真实。

speaker: Unknown
en: So in his biography, he has this line.
zh: 所以在他的传记中，他有这样一句话。

speaker: Unknown
en: People ask him, "What's it like to be famous?"
zh: 人们问他，“出名是什么感觉？”

speaker: Unknown
en: And his answer is, "Becoming famous is amazing.
zh: 他的回答是，“变得出名是惊人的。

speaker: Unknown
en: Being famous is a mixed bag.
zh: 保持有名是喜忧参半的。

speaker: Unknown
en: Losing fame is miserable."
zh: 失去名气是悲惨的。”

speaker: Unknown
en: That's funny.
zh: 这很有趣。

speaker: Unknown
en: So first of all, I think a lot of people are experiencing this right now.
zh: 首先，我认为很多人现在正在经历这种情况。

speaker: Unknown
en: You have a lot of companies that have reasonable products and their growth has slowed.
zh: 你有很多拥有合理产品的公司，它们的增长已经放缓。

speaker: Unknown
en: Why?
zh: 为什么？

speaker: Unknown
en: It could be the economy because it's not as good as a lot of indicators say.
zh: 可能是经济，因为它不像很多指标说的那么好。

speaker: Unknown
en: We all know that, for example, jobs are not as good as the indicators say.
zh: 我们都知道，例如，就业不像指标说的那么好。

speaker: Unknown
en: It could be because AI or the threat of AI or the expectation of AI, blah, blah, blah.
zh: 可能是因为 AI 或 AI 的威胁或对 AI 的期望，等等，等等，等等。

speaker: Unknown
en: Who knows?
zh: 谁知道呢？

speaker: Unknown
en: It also can just be size.
zh: 也可能只是因为规模。

speaker: Unknown
en: As you get bigger, growth slows because you know what?
zh: 随着你变大，增长放缓，因为你知道为什么吗？

speaker: Unknown
en: You're not going to grow 2X a year forever.
zh: 你不可能永远每年增长 2 倍。

speaker: Unknown
en: So it slows.
zh: 所以它放缓了。

speaker: Unknown
en: There's just mechanical things.
zh: 这只是机械性的问题。

speaker: Unknown
en: So there's many reasons why things slow.
zh: 所以事情放缓有很多原因。

speaker: Unknown
en: And sometimes it's all of a sudden, although then maybe there's some event like an algorithm changes or something happens.
zh: 有时它是突然发生的，尽管那样可能会有一些事件，比如算法改变或者发生了什么。

speaker: Unknown
en: But actually, I think what's really common is it just slowly gets slower.
zh: 但实际上，我认为真正常见的是它只是缓慢地变慢。

speaker: Unknown
en: In other words, it decelerates.
zh: 换句话说，它在减速。

speaker: Unknown
en: But I wouldn't say sneaks up on you because most people are looking at growth all the time, so it's not sneaky, but it is sort of a little bit more gradual where just like, you just feel more like you're running through mud like, "Ah, God, we're just still doing so much work and it's not having as much of an impact."
zh: 但我不会说它会悄悄逼近你，因为大多数人一直在看增长，所以它不隐秘，但它确实有点更渐进，就像，你只是感觉更像你在泥浆中奔跑，像，“啊，天哪，我们仍然在做这么多工作，但它没有产生那么大的影响。”

speaker: Unknown
en: And so that's what I see.
zh: 所以这就是我所看到的。

speaker: Unknown
en: And when I say that's what I see, so I've built four companies.
zh: 当我说那是我的所见时，所以我建立过四家公司。

speaker: Unknown
en: The last one is a unicorn.
zh: 最后一家是独角兽。

speaker: Unknown
en: The one before that is also a unicorn.
zh: 前一家也是独角兽。

speaker: Unknown
en: The previous one was bootstrapped.
zh: 再前一家是自举的。

speaker: Unknown
en: This one was VC-funded and I've invested in about 60 startups.
zh: 这一家是风投支持的，我投资了大约 60 家初创公司。

speaker: Unknown
en: Some of them failed completely.
zh: 其中一些彻底失败了。

speaker: Unknown
en: Some of them were very successful, some in the middle, because of course, right?
zh: 其中一些非常成功，一些处于中间，因为当然是这样，对吧？

speaker: Unknown
en: And so when I say that's what I've seen, that's the context of what I mean by what I've seen.
zh: 所以当我说那是我的所见时，这就是我所说的“我的所见”的背景。

speaker: Unknown
en: So there's, I wouldn't say a checklist, but there's a series of questions that I asked to diagnose why is growth slowing in this order, because it's one of these things where the first one that's a problem, if you don't fix that, it doesn't matter if you fix one of the ones below.
zh: 所以这里有一个，我不会说是一个清单，但有一系列问题我按这个顺序提出来诊断为什么增长放缓，因为这是那种事情，即如果是第一个有问题，如果你不修复那个，你修复下面任何一个都没有用。

speaker: Unknown
en: Just like if, I don't know, maybe if you had a marketing funnel and there's a step where everything falls apart and you're like, "Well, I'll just tune the bottom of it a little."
zh: 就像如果，我不知道，也许如果你有一个营销漏斗，其中有一一步 everything 分崩离析，然后你说，“好吧，我就稍微调整一下底部。”

speaker: Unknown
en: It's like, "That's not going to work.
zh: 就像，“那是行不通的。”

speaker: Unknown
en: It's not going to help enough.
zh: 它不会有什么帮助。

speaker: Unknown
en: You got to go where the biggest issue is."
zh: 你得去最大的问题所在的地方。”

speaker: Unknown
en: So this is in that sort of order.
zh: 所以这就是按那个顺序。

speaker: Unknown
en: So the first question is are customers leaving, i.e.
zh: 所以第一个问题是客户在离开吗，即。

speaker: Unknown
en: logo churn, right?
zh: 标志流失，对吧？

speaker: Unknown
en: Churn within.
zh: 内部的流失。

speaker: Unknown
en: You can do churn with MRR too, but just for simplicity, let's say with customers.
zh: 你也可以用 MRR 计算流失，但为了简单起见，我们就说是客户。

speaker: Unknown
en: And it's the worst problem for a couple reasons.
zh: 这是一个最糟糕的问题，有几个原因。

speaker: Unknown
en: One is there's nothing you can do about it once it happens.
zh: 一是一旦发生了，你对此无能为力。

speaker: Unknown
en: They're gone.
zh: 他们走了。

speaker: Unknown
en: There's no saving them, increasing their revenue.
zh: 没有拯救他们的办法，增加他们的收入。

speaker: Unknown
en: There's nothing in the future you can do.
zh: 将来你也没什么事可做。

speaker: Unknown
en: Also, it's often correlated with things like negative reviews or other things on social media, which is another kind of preventing growth.
zh: 此外，它通常与负面评论或社交媒体上的其他事情相关，这是另一种阻碍增长的方式。

speaker: Unknown
en: So it's kind of a two punch thing of like, they're not here and they may be actively hurting your growth, so that sucks.
zh: 所以这有点像双重打击，就像，他们不在这里，而且他们可能在主动伤害你的增长，所以那很糟糕。

speaker: Unknown
en: The math is undeniable, which I want to talk about because this is something where there's a metric I like that is unusual and people find useful.
zh: 数学上是不可否认的，我想谈谈这一点，因为这里有一个我喜欢的不寻常的指标，人们觉得很有用。

speaker: Unknown
en: But before I get to the metric, there's also this kind of visceral thing, which is the customer's saying, "This product, I don't want it."
zh: 但在我讲到那个指标之前，也有这种发自内心的事，即客户在说，“这个产品，我不想要它。”

speaker: Unknown
en: And when I think about the gauntlet they went through to get to the product, how did they even find out about me?
zh: 当我想想他们为获得产品所经历的 gauntlet，他们到底是怎么发现我的？

speaker: Unknown
en: That was hard already and improbable that they see an ad or hear it.
zh: 那已经很难了，并且不太可能他们看到广告或听说了它。

speaker: Unknown
en: And then they clicked, which is improbable, and then they didn't just bounce off the homepage, which is again, improbable.
zh: 然后他们点击了，这不太可能，然后他们没有只是跳出首页，这又不太可能。

speaker: Unknown
en: They actually were like, "Oh yeah, this sounds pretty good."
zh: 他们实际上像，“哦，是的，这听起来很好。”

speaker: Unknown
en: And then they got to the pricing page and that didn't scare them off.
zh: 然后他们到了定价页面，那并没有把他们吓跑。

speaker: Unknown
en: They actually had the budget and bought the stupid thing.
zh: 他们实际上有预算并买了那个愚蠢的东西。

speaker: Unknown
en: Then they went through onboarding and invested their time, et cetera, et cetera.
zh: 然后他们经历了 onboarding 并投入了他们的时间，等等，等等。

speaker: Unknown
en: That is a crazy gauntlet that almost no one gets through.
zh: 这是一个疯狂的 gauntlet，几乎没有人能通过。

speaker: Unknown
en: And after all of that, which clearly means they wanted it to work, they're like, "No, bye."
zh: 在所有这一切之后，这显然意味着他们希望它奏效，他们却说，“不，拜拜。”

speaker: Unknown
en: What?
zh: 什么？

speaker: Unknown
en: Just on an emotional level, you got to go, "Wait a minute, that's terrible.
zh: 就在情感层面上，你必须说，“等一下，这太糟糕了。

speaker: Unknown
en: I'm fundamentally not fulfilling whatever promise I made or they thought I made, which is whether that's a product issue or a communication issue."
zh: 我根本上没有履行我做出的任何承诺或他们认为我做出的承诺，不管是产品问题还是沟通问题。”

speaker: Unknown
en: Okay.
zh: 好的。

speaker: Unknown
en: There's lots of... But one way or another, something is really fundamentally broken just in terms of like, I'm a product person, so what I want to do is make a product that other people want to buy and use.
zh: 很多……但无论如何，从某种意义上说确实有些东西从根本上坏了，就像，我是一个做产品的人，所以我想要做的是做出其他人想要购买和使用的产品。

speaker: Unknown
en: And if they don't, no matter what the metrics say, we're failing our mission, our customers, whatever.
zh: 如果他们不买不管指标说什么，我们在使命、客户等等方面都失败了。

speaker: Unknown
en: So there's just even that non-mathematical reason to go, "Oh my God."
zh: 所以甚至有那个非数学的原因让人去说，“天哪。”

speaker: Unknown
en: Right?
zh: 对吧？

speaker: Unknown
en: So to me, that's already enough reason, but the math is very interesting.
zh: 所以对我来说，这已经是足够的理由了，但这数学很有趣。

speaker: Unknown
en: And what I find is when I talk to people, especially on Twitter or something where people are just yapping around whatever they're doing, I say things like anything above 3% per month cancellation is terrible.
zh: 我发现的是当我跟人交谈时，特别是在 Twitter 或者某些人们只是围着他们在做的事闲扯的地方，我会说比如任何高于每月 3% 的取消率都很糟糕。

speaker: Unknown
en: And people are like, "Oh no, it's okay.
zh: 然后人们就说，“不，这没关系。

speaker: Unknown
en: Five is fine, seven, six."

speaker: Unknown
en: Everyone's yapping about what they... And it's very abstract.
zh: 每个人都在闲扯他们的……而且这很抽象。

speaker: Unknown
en: Is four much worse than five?
zh: 4 比 5 差很多吗？

speaker: Unknown
en: I don't know.
zh: 我不知道。

speaker: Unknown
en: And I heard someone else, and blah, blah, blah.
zh: 我听到别人说过，还有巴拉巴拉。

speaker: Unknown
en: So it's very, I don't know, generic and rough.
zh: 所以这很，我不知道，通用的和粗略的。

speaker: Unknown
en: So there's a different metric that I like to use, which keys off of this idea that I think, again, people don't appreciate, which is cancellations grow faster than marketing, and so cancellations overpower the growth of the company and slow it to a halt, i.e.
zh: 所以我有一个我喜欢使用的不同的指标，它基于这个我认为再次人们不欣赏的想法，也就是取消比营销增长得更快，所以取消压倒了公司的增长并让它慢下来直到停止，也就是。

speaker: Unknown
en: growth slows, right?
zh: 增长放缓，对吧？

speaker: Unknown
en: To where you literally cannot grow anymore.
zh: 直到你真的无法再增长。

speaker: Unknown
en: There's a maximum ceiling of how big you could ever be thanks to cancellations.
zh: 有一个你原本能有多大上限的最大值，多亏了取消。

speaker: Unknown
en: And when you know what that number is, it's much more real and visceral and scary.
zh: 当你知道那个数字是什么时，它更真实、发自内心且可怕。

speaker: Unknown
en: And so just to kind of justify what I just said, just imagine any company and imagine you just tripled the number of customers that are there and paying, and the same kind, the same age, just the same kind of stuff just tripled overnight.
zh: 所以只是为我刚才说的做辩护，想象任何公司并想象你只是把在那里的付费客户数量翻了两番，同样的种类，同样的年龄，只是同样的东西只是过夜翻了两番。

speaker: Unknown
en: So the next month, would marketing deliver more new customers than a month before?
zh: 所以下个月，营销会带来比前一个月更多的新客户吗？

speaker: Unknown
en: No, because none of your marketing efforts care how many customers you have.
zh: 不，因为你的营销努力没有一个在乎你有多少客户。

speaker: Unknown
en: AdWords delivers the same number of leads and SEO delivers the same.
zh: AdWords 提供相同数量的线索，SEO 也是。

speaker: Unknown
en: It does not care how big you are, these efforts.
zh: 这些努力不在乎你有多大。

speaker: Unknown
en: So you're still going to be growing at the same rate as you were the previous month.
zh: 所以你仍然会以和上个月相同的速率增长。

speaker: Unknown
en: But cancellations in absolute terms, like the number of customers who leave will triple because you have 5% cancellation and triple... Okay, so still 5% of a triple number is triple, right?
zh: 但取消的绝对数量，像离开的客户数量将翻两番，因为你有 5% 的取消率并且翻两番……好的，所以翻两番的数字的 5% 仍然是翻两番，对吧？

speaker: Unknown
en: So this is the point, is that cancellations automatically grow as you grow, even if you're doing everything right, but marketing doesn't.
zh: 所以这就是重点，也就是取消随着你增长自动增长，即使你做的一切都对的，但营销不会。

speaker: Unknown
en: Marketing grows only as fast as you can improve marketing.
zh: 营销增长的速度只和你能改进营销的速度一样快。

speaker: Unknown
en: We all know that's quite hard actually.
zh: 我们都知道这实际上很难。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's linear.
zh: 它是线性的。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's hard to find new channels that aren't trivial.
zh: 很难找到新的不微不足道的渠道。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's hard.
zh: 很难。

speaker: Unknown
en: And of course we're going to do it, but it's hard, whereas cancellations grow automatically as you grow.
zh: 当然我们会去做，但这很难，然而取消随着你增长自动增长。

speaker: Unknown
en: So cancellations always overtake marketing for this reason.
zh: 所以因为这个原因取消总是超过营销。

speaker: Unknown
en: The metaphor here is a leaky bucket where are you adding enough water to keep up with the leak essentially?
zh: 这里的隐喻是一个漏水的桶，你基本上在加足够的水来跟上漏水的速度吗？

speaker: Unknown
en: Right.
zh: 对。

speaker: Unknown
en: Except the leaks automatically increase and that's what people don't appreciate.
zh: 除了漏水自动增加，这就是人们不欣赏的地方。

speaker: Unknown
en: Because it's a percentage of your entire customer base.
zh: 因为它是你整个客户群的一个百分比。

speaker: Unknown
en: Yes.
zh: 是的。

speaker: Unknown
en: So we say... When in marketing, we say things like, "I'm adding a hundred leads a month."
zh: 所以我们说……在营销中，我们说像，“我一个月增加 100 个线索。”

speaker: Unknown
en: But in cancellations, we say 5%.
zh: 但在取消方面，我们说 5%。

speaker: Unknown
en: Why'd you say percent?
zh: 你为什么说百分比？

speaker: Unknown
en: Because it's based on your size and it's exponential.
zh: 因为它是基于你的大小并且它是指数的。

speaker: Unknown
en: That's what 5% is, an exponential.
zh: 这就是 5% 是什么，一个指数。

speaker: Unknown
en: And so there's this maximum size you could ever be.
zh: 所以有一个你原本能有多大的最大尺寸。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's when churn equals growth.
zh: 那就是当流失等于增长时。

speaker: Unknown
en: So how would you compute that?
zh: 那么你会怎么计算那个？

speaker: Unknown
en: It's actually quite simple because let's say you have this 5% per month, just let's take a number.
zh: 这其实很简单，因为假设你有这个每月 5%，就让我们拿一个数字。

speaker: Unknown
en: So it's simply the amount of new customers you add divided by that cancellation rate.
zh: 所以它只是你增加的新客户的数量除以那个取消率。

speaker: Unknown
en: That is the amount.
zh: 那就是这个数量。

speaker: Unknown
en: That is the limit.
zh: 那就是极限。

speaker: Unknown
en: So let's suppose you add 100 customers a month and you have 5% cancellation.
zh: 所以让我们假设你每个月增加 100 个客户，并且你有 5% 的取消率。

speaker: Unknown
en: So 100 divided by 5% is 2,000.

speaker: Unknown
en: So a company like that will never have more than 2,000 customers.
zh: 所以像这样的公司永远不会有超过 2,000 个客户。

speaker: Unknown
en: And by the way, as you approach that number, growth is very slow because you bring in a bunch of customers and almost the same number leave, so growth is slowing.
zh: 顺便说一句，当你接近那个数字时，增长是非常缓慢的，因为你带来了一批客户，而且几乎相同数量的客户离开，所以增长在放缓。

speaker: Unknown
en: Ah, look, we've diagnosed by growth slows automatically at all SaaS companies.
zh: 啊，看，我们已经诊断出所有 SaaS 公司的增长都会自动放缓。

speaker: Unknown
en: So that's why this is the first thing, because it's such a hard cap limit and it means that people don't want your product.
zh: 所以这就是为什么这是第一件事，因为它是这样一个硬性上限限制，这意味着人们不想要你的产品。

speaker: Unknown
en: These are two reasons why it's the most important thing.
zh: 这是两个原因，说明为什么它是最重要的事情。

speaker: Unknown
en: Just to clarify, this is logo churn.
zh: 只是为了澄清，这是 logo 流失。

speaker: Unknown
en: This is like number of customers, not revenue churn?
zh: 这就像客户数量，而不是收入流失？

speaker: Unknown
en: Yeah.
zh: 是的。

speaker: Unknown
en: Well, it is both logo churn and revenue churn.
zh: 嗯，它既是 logo 流失也是收入流失。

speaker: Unknown
en: Do the same math.
zh: 做同样的数学计算。

speaker: Unknown
en: You could say dollars in divided by dollars cancellation rate or number of... I've been saying number of customers just to keep it simple because I think when you look at it and say, "Wow, we will never have more than 2,000 customers."
zh: 你可以说美元流入除以美元取消率或数量……我一直说客户数量只是为了保持简单，因为我认为当你看着它并说，“哇，我们永远不会有超过 2,000 个客户。”

speaker: Unknown
en: It's just such a visceral, "Oh my God, we got to do something about that."
zh: 这只是一种发自内心的反应，“天哪，我们必须对此做点什么。”

speaker: Unknown
en: Now, of course, one thing you could do is have more marketing, but you know that already.
zh: 现在，当然，你可以做的一件事是做更多的营销，但你已经知道那个了。

speaker: Unknown
en: If growth is slowing, you're already thinking, how do I get more out of marketing?
zh: 如果增长放缓，你已经在思考，我如何从营销中获得更多？

speaker: Unknown
en: You knew that.
zh: 你知道那个。

speaker: Unknown
en: The point is that cancellation is this hard limit pulling you down with all these other really bad either implications or side effects, which is why it's so important.
zh: 关键在于，取消率是这个硬性限制，把你往下拉，伴随着所有这些其他真正坏的要么是暗示要么是副作用，这就是为什么它如此重要。

speaker: Unknown
en: Cool.
zh: 很酷。

speaker: Unknown
en: And when you say marketing, just to clarify, this includes basically all growth work, PLG stuff, marketing, sales?
zh: 当你说营销时，为了澄清，这基本上包括所有增长工作，PLG 东西，营销，销售？

speaker: Unknown
en: Yeah.
zh: 是的。

speaker: Unknown
en: Yeah, yeah.
zh: 是的，是的。

speaker: Unknown
en: Right.
zh: 对。

speaker: Unknown
en: Great.
zh: 很好。

speaker: Unknown
en: Yeah.
zh: 是的。

speaker: Unknown
en: PLG is nice, but you still need marketing to bring the people in the first place.
zh: PLG 很好，但你仍然需要营销来首先把人带进来。

speaker: Unknown
en: PLG just means there's not a salesperson unless you're expanding or some other segment.
zh: PLG 只是意味着没有销售人员，除非你在扩张或其他细分市场。

speaker: Unknown
en: Cool.
zh: 酷。

speaker: Unknown
en: Yeah.
zh: 是的。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's like the whole bucket of just bringing new customers in.
zh: 这就像整个桶，只是把新客户带进来。

speaker: Unknown
en: Awesome.
zh: 很棒。

speaker: Unknown
en: Yeah, yeah.
zh: 是的，是的。

speaker: Unknown
en: Okay.
zh: 好的。

speaker: Unknown
en: So assuming you agree, yeah, I don't like customers leaving, that sucks, so obviously you got to find out why they're canceling and do something about it.
zh: 所以假设你同意，是的，我不喜欢客户离开，那很糟糕，所以显然你必须找出他们取消的原因并对此做点什么。

speaker: Unknown
en: And the kind of root issue here is they don't want to tell you.
zh: 这里的根本问题是他们不想告诉你。

speaker: Unknown
en: They're already out the door.
zh: 他们已经出门了。

speaker: Unknown
en: They've already stopped investing in you mentally.
zh: 他们已经在精神上停止对你投资了。

speaker: Unknown
en: So the last thing I want to do is spend time with you or really think about it and diagnose it with you.
zh: 所以我最不想做的事就是花时间和你在一起，或者真正思考这个问题并与你一起诊断。

speaker: Unknown
en: And I have a funny story about this for myself.
zh: 我有一个关于这点的有趣故事。

speaker: Unknown
en: So at SmartBear, people would cancel, we'd put up this form and a dropdown list, too expensive, project ended, this little stuff like we do, so we could gather data.
zh: 所以在 SmartBear，人们会取消，我们会弹出这个表单和一个下拉列表，太贵了，项目结束了，这些小事情就像我们做的那样，所以我们可以收集数据。

speaker: Unknown
en: And one of them did have more selection than the rest.
zh: 其中一个确实比其他的有更多选择。

speaker: Unknown
en: And I realized it was the first one on the list.
zh: 我意识到它是列表上的第一个。

speaker: Unknown
en: And I thought, huh, I wonder if people are just picking the first one.
zh: 我想，嗯，我想知道人们是否只是选择第一个。

speaker: Unknown
en: So then we randomized the list so everyone saw a different order of the list, and now all the items were picked equally, like, oh, right.
zh: 然后我们随机化了列表，所以每个人看到的列表顺序都不同，现在所有的项目都被平等地选择了，就像，哦，对。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's complete noise.
zh: 这完全是噪音。

speaker: Unknown
en: And I know other companies have done similar things also with the similar results that this is a global phenomenon.
zh: 我知道其他公司也做过类似的事情，也有类似的结果，这是一个全球现象。

speaker: Unknown
en: So okay, so what do you do?
zh: 所以好吧，那么你做什么？

speaker: Unknown
en: The point is, it's hard.
zh: 关键是，这很难。

speaker: Unknown
en: So the first thing is you want to ask open-ended questions.
zh: 所以第一件事是你想问开放式问题。

speaker: Unknown
en: I know you want to just get a list, but this is the problem.
zh: 我知道你只是想得到一个列表，但这就是问题所在。

speaker: Unknown
en: At least with open-ended questions, I mean, most people won't answer, but at least you might be able to get some kind of thing that they generated.
zh: 至少对于开放式问题,我的意思是,大多数人不会回答,但至少你也许能获得他们生成的某种东西。

speaker: Unknown
en: And when you do this, the wrong way is to ask, why did you cancel?
zh: 当你这样做时,错误的方式是问,你为什么取消?

speaker: Unknown
en: Because again, this allows them to say something really simple like budget, which may or may not be true.
zh: 因为同样地,这允许他们说一些非常简单的话,比如预算,这可能是真的也可能不是。

speaker: Unknown
en: I'll get to that in a second.
zh: 我稍后会讲到那个。

speaker: Unknown
en: What you want to do is say, "What made you cancel?"
zh: 你想做的是说,「是什么让你取消?」

speaker: Unknown
en: In other words, what about the product or situation or whatever caused the cancellation?
zh: 换句话说,产品或情况或其他什么导致了取消?

speaker: Unknown
en: Just phrasing it that way, you get much better results.
zh: 只是这样措辞,你会得到更好的结果。

speaker: Unknown
en: And I stole this from a company called Groove, who has this great case study online about this very thing.
zh: 我是从一家叫 Groove 的公司偷学来的,他们在网上有一个关于这件事的很棒的案例研究。

speaker: Unknown
en: They had an email that they sent out, which is a great email, and they started by asking, "Why did you cancel?"
zh: 他们有一封发送出去的邮件,那是一封很棒的邮件,他们开始时问,「你为什么取消?」

speaker: Unknown
en: They got 10% usable responses.
zh: 他们得到了 10% 的可用回复。

speaker: Unknown
en: They changed the same email to what made you cancel, and it's 20% usable responses.
zh: 他们将同样的邮件改成了是什么让你取消,这就是 20% 的可用回复。

speaker: Unknown
en: So there's, I guess, maybe some anecdata that this is a good idea.
zh: 所以我想,也许有一些轶事数据表明这是个好主意。

speaker: Unknown
en: But the point is you really want them thinking about the product and not just coming up with an excuse.
zh: 但关键是你真的希望他们思考产品,而不只是想出一个借口。

speaker: Unknown
en: The next thing is the few times you do talk to them, so you want to go into, delve as far as you can into there, because most people won't talk, the temptation is to hear what they generate at first and say, "That's the answer."
zh: 接下来是,少数几次你确实与他们交谈,所以你想要尽可能深入地钻研那里,因为大多数人不会交谈,诱惑就在于听取他们最初生成的内容并说,「这就是答案。」

speaker: Unknown
en: So a really common one is it's too expensive.
zh: 所以一个非常常见的例子是它太贵了。

speaker: Unknown
en: I think anyone who's looked at cancellation data at any company will agree that too expensive is often the number one or at least top three reason in one form or another.
zh: 我想任何看过任何公司取消数据的人都会同意,太贵了通常是头号原因,或者至少是前三名原因之一,以某种形式。

speaker: Unknown
en: And that is never, ever, ever the reason.
zh: 那绝不是,永远,永远,永远不是原因。

speaker: Unknown
en: How do I know?
zh: 我怎么知道?

speaker: Unknown
en: Because they already looked at your homepage, read all the stuff, saw what you promised, looked at the pricing page and decided to buy it.
zh: 因为他们已经看过你的主页,读过所有东西,看过你承诺的内容,看过定价页面并决定购买它。

speaker: Unknown
en: That means it, whatever was in their mind of what it is, is not too expensive.
zh: 这意味着它,无论他们心中认为是什么,都不算太贵。

speaker: Unknown
en: They already decided with their actions.
zh: 他们已经用行动决定了。

speaker: Unknown
en: It was not too expensive.
zh: 它并不太贵。

speaker: Unknown
en: Something else happened, but you didn't fulfill the promise that at least they thought you made or something else didn't work or... Now, it is possible they lost budget, but that doesn't mean you're too expensive.
zh: 发生了别的事情,但你没有兑现承诺,至少是他们认为你做出的承诺,或者别的东西不起作用或者…… 现在,他们确实可能失去了预算,但这并不意味着你太贵了。

speaker: Unknown
en: That means they lost budget.
zh: 这意味着他们失去了预算。

speaker: Unknown
en: That's a very different reason.
zh: 那是一个非常不同的原因。

speaker: Unknown
en: So it's sort of like, this happens in healthcare, for example.
zh: 所以这有点像,这发生在医疗保健中,例如。

speaker: Unknown
en: So when someone dies, a doctor has to write what's called a proximate cause, which is why did they actually die?
zh: 所以当某人去世时,医生必须写下所谓的近因,也就是他们为什么实际上死了?

speaker: Unknown
en: But then you try to also write down the real reason.
zh: 但然后你也试着写下真正的理由。

speaker: Unknown
en: So let's say someone comes in and the proximate cause of death is they stop breathing.
zh: 所以假设某人进来了,死亡的直接近因是他们停止了呼吸。

speaker: Unknown
en: Well, you could stop there and that's like listening to it's expensive and going, "That's it."
zh: 好吧,你可以停在那里,这就像听人说它太贵了然后说,「就是这样。」

speaker: Unknown
en: Well, why did they stop breathing?
zh: 好吧,为什么他们停止了呼吸?

speaker: Unknown
en: Because they ran their car into a telephone pole and were injured so much that eventually they stopped breathing.
zh: 因为他们把车撞上了电线杆,受伤太重导致最终停止了呼吸。

speaker: Unknown
en: Why did they run their car into a telephone pole?
zh: 为什么他们把车撞上了电线杆?

speaker: Unknown
en: Because they passed out at the wheel.
zh: 因为他们在驾驶时晕过去了。

speaker: Unknown
en: Why did they pass out at the wheel?
zh: 为什么他们在驾驶时晕过去了?

speaker: Unknown
en: Because they had undiagnosed diabetes.
zh: 因为他们患有未确诊的糖尿病。

speaker: Unknown
en: Now we're getting somewhere.
zh: 现在我们有点头绪了。

speaker: Unknown
en: It still isn't just one root cause, another as a sidebar.
zh: 它仍然不仅仅是一个根本原因,另一个作为旁注。

speaker: Unknown
en: I hate the idea of a root cause.
zh: 我讨厌根本原因这个想法。

speaker: Unknown
en: Complex systems do not have one root cause.
zh: 复杂系统没有单一的根本原因。

speaker: Unknown
en: They often have many interlocking things that could be done to detect earlier or to change it or to reduce and not one root cause.
zh: 它们通常有许多相互关联的事情可以做,以便更早检测或改变它或减少,而不是一个根本原因。

speaker: Unknown
en: So the root cause analysis to me is, by the way, an incorrect thing.
zh: 所以对我来说,根本原因分析顺便说一句是不正确的事情。

speaker: Unknown
en: I'm explaining why right now with the healthcare, right?
zh: 我现在用医疗保健的例子在解释为什么,对吧?

speaker: Unknown
en: Because, well, what about this undiagnosed?
zh: 因为,好吧,那这个未确诊的呢?

speaker: Unknown
en: Well, maybe part of the problem is we have a healthcare system that isn't preventative and part of it is that, but they didn't go to the doctor anyway.
zh: 好吧,也许问题的一部分是我们有一个非预防性的医疗保健系统,一部分是那个,但他们反正也没去看医生。

speaker: Unknown
en: Okay, so there's all kinds of things that could be useful and interesting to prevent this or make it better.
zh: 好吧,所以有各种各样的事情可能有用且有趣,可以预防这种情况或让它变得更好。

speaker: Unknown
en: That's the point.
zh: 这就是重点。

speaker: Unknown
en: That's what an analysis should be, is this array of things, not the root cause.
zh: 这就是分析应该是什么,是这个事物阵列,而不是根本原因。

speaker: Unknown
en: Anyway, something along the lines of undiagnosed diabetes is much more of a cause than stopped breathing.
zh: 无论如何,类似于未确诊的糖尿病这种东西比停止呼吸更是一个原因。

speaker: Unknown
en: So when we say it's too expensive and that's the reason, you're making this fallacy, you got to go into, well, they wanted this stuff, but it didn't work with Linear, which is what they use.
zh: 所以当我们说它太贵了而这就是原因时,你正在犯这个谬误,你必须深入研究,好吧,他们想要这些东西,但它不适用于 Linear,也就是他们用的东西。

speaker: Unknown
en: It only works with Jira.
zh: 它只适用于 Jira。

speaker: Unknown
en: And so there's a lack of integration.
zh: 因此缺乏集成。

speaker: Unknown
en: Now, maybe we should write that integration and maybe we shouldn't.
zh: 现在，也许我们应该编写那个集成，也许我们不应该。

speaker: Unknown
en: Of course, it depends on how much we hear about it.
zh: 当然，这取决于我们对此听到了多少。

speaker: Unknown
en: And of course it's going to depend on other things, but that's the reason, not it is expensive, right?
zh: 当然，这也取决于其他事情，但这就是原因，并不是因为它贵，对吧？

speaker: Unknown
en: And so this idea of getting into not even the root cause, but let's say root-er causes.
zh: 因此，这种深入甚至不是根本原因，但让我们说更根本原因的想法。

speaker: Unknown
en: The roots cause.
zh: 根本原因。

speaker: Unknown
en: Yeah, the more root.
zh: 是的，更根本。

speaker: Unknown
en: I think some people probably say five whys and just paper over what I just said with that.
zh: 我认为有些人可能会说「五个为什么」，并只是用它来掩盖我刚才说的话。

speaker: Unknown
en: And maybe so, but let's not be so simplistic about that, because again, five why sometimes implies that there's some root cause at the bottom of the whys.
zh: 也许是这样，但让我们不要太简单化，因为同样，「五个为什么」有时暗示在为什么的最底部有某种根本原因。

speaker: Unknown
en: Let's be a little more smart about that.
zh: 让我们在那方面更聪明一点。

speaker: Unknown
en: So anyway, these things too expensive, this is not it.
zh: 所以总之，这些东西太贵了，这不是原因。

speaker: Unknown
en: Maybe project ended really is project ended.
zh: 也许「项目结束」真的就是项目结束了。

speaker: Unknown
en: Okay.
zh: 好的。

speaker: Unknown
en: But even there, I see just today, today on an entrepreneur forum I'm on, someone said, "Yeah, we're starting to see more people have project ended as the reason, and so there's nothing we can do about that."
zh: 但即使在那里，我就在今天，就在今天在我参与的一个创业者论坛上，有人说，「是的，我们开始看到更多人将『项目结束』作为原因，所以我们对此无能为力。」

speaker: Unknown
en: Now see, that's incorrect.
zh: 现在看，这是不正确的。

speaker: Unknown
en: That's only true if you only look at the proximate thing, which is project ended.
zh: 只有当你只看最近的事情，即项目结束时，这才是真的。

speaker: Unknown
en: You're correct that you can't make that project not end exactly.
zh: 你无法让那个项目不结束是没错的。

speaker: Unknown
en: Yeah.
zh: 是的。

speaker: Unknown
en: Okay, but wait a minute.
zh: 好的，但是等一下。

speaker: Unknown
en: If your software was more successful and the project was more successful, would it have ended or is that actually an indicator that your product wasn't that useful or didn't do its job?
zh: 如果你的软件更成功，项目也更成功，它会结束吗，或者这实际上表明你的产品没那么有用或没有完成它的工作？

speaker: Unknown
en: It's possible, like in this case, who knows, right?
zh: 有可能，就像在这个例子中，谁知道呢，对吧？

speaker: Unknown
en: But that's possible that it really is your fault.
zh: 但这确实可能是你的错。

speaker: Unknown
en: Another example is, but you picked what target segments you are going after.
zh: 另一个例子是，但你选择了你追求的目标细分市场。

speaker: Unknown
en: Did you pick a market segment that was easier to sell to, but their projects end like small business and consumers where very often the small business does go out of business where the project ends, et cetera?
zh: 你是否选择了一个更容易销售的市场细分，但他们的项目像小企业和消费者那样结束了，而在这些领域，小企业经常倒闭，项目结束等等？

speaker: Unknown
en: Because when things are small, they have high variance and lots of things can knock them off the path and so on.
zh: 因为当事情很小时，它们具有很高的方差，很多事情可以让它们偏离路径等等。

speaker: Unknown
en: And so is it your fault for picking the wrong ideal customer profile or target segment?
zh: 那么，选择错误的理想客户画像或目标细分市场是你的错吗？

speaker: Unknown
en: And so yes, that one case of that one project, that's not your fault, quote-unquote, but by saying that you're just ignoring the fact that there is maybe something to do about it... Now, all this is maybe.
zh: 所以是的，那个项目的那个案例，那不是你的错，所谓的，但那样说你就忽略了可能对此有所作为的事实……现在，这都是也许。

speaker: Unknown
en: None of this proves you should change your market, but when you say there's nothing we can do about it, you are closing the door on these things that might be the right thing.
zh: 这都不证明你应该改变你的市场，但当你对此无能为力时，你正在关闭这些可能是正确的事情的大门。

speaker: Unknown
en: And very often, as I think probably a lot of people here on this listening to this know, the market segment you pick has a lot to do with your retention rate because everyone acts differently.
zh: 而且通常，正如我想这里的很多人都知道的那样，你选择的市场细分与你的留存率有很大关系，因为每个人的行为都不同。

speaker: Unknown
en: And so anyway, so I know it's a lot on this topic, but I just feel constantly, people make this particular mistake of just abdicating responsibility or just listening to the first thing they hear and saying that's the reason and that's not right.
zh: 所以总之，我知道关于这个话题说了很多，但我只是觉得，人们不断地犯这个特定的错误，只是推卸责任或只是听到的第一件事并说这就是原因，这是不对的。

speaker: Unknown
en: So that's the big thing about listening.
zh: 所以这就是关于倾听的大事。

speaker: Unknown
en: Another thing is you got to ask when people are in trouble, but not yet canceled.
zh: 另一件事是，你必须询问人们何时遇到麻烦，但还没取消。

speaker: Unknown
en: You might be able to save them.
zh: 你也许能挽救他们。

speaker: Unknown
en: You certainly can learn more because you can talk to them.
zh: 你肯定能学到更多，因为你可以和他们交谈。

speaker: Unknown
en: They're not shut off yet from you.
zh: 他们还没有切断与你的联系。

speaker: Unknown
en: So this might be, they never uploaded their data, so they're not being successful.
zh: 所以这可能是，他们从未上传他们的数据，所以他们没有成功。

speaker: Unknown
en: They are calling tech support too much.
zh: 他们打技术支持电话太频繁了。

speaker: Unknown
en: They're in trouble.
zh: 他们遇到麻烦了。

speaker: Unknown
en: They're not calling tech support enough.
zh: 他们打技术支持电话不够。

speaker: Unknown
en: They're not engaged.
zh: 他们没有参与。

speaker: Unknown
en: They didn't log in for a while.
zh: 他们有一段时间没有登录了。

speaker: Unknown
en: There's all kinds of things where... Now, of course, the details are going to depend on the product, obviously, but there are signals that are correlated with cancellation.
zh: 各种各样的情况……现在，当然，细节显然取决于产品，但有与取消相关的信号。

speaker: Unknown
en: Now, if you have a lot of data, you can literally correlate signals with cancellation and try to extract that precisely, but even without data, you can guess.
zh: 现在，如果你有大量数据，你实际上可以将信号与取消联系起来，并尝试精确提取它，但即使没有数据，你也可以猜测。

speaker: Unknown
en: And guessing and having a theory, acting accordingly, and as you get more data adjusting your theory, this is a wise way to proceed even without data.
zh: 猜测并有一个理论，据此行动，并在你获得更多数据时调整你的理论，这是一个明智的前进方式，即使没有数据。

speaker: Unknown
en: So if you can catch them when they seem like they're off the happy path, they're in trouble, that's a better time to do it.
zh: 所以，如果你能在他们似乎偏离了快乐路径、遇到麻烦时抓住他们，那是更好的时机。

speaker: Unknown
en: And then the last thing I would say about this detection is if you don't know what to do or all else being equal, then focus-
zh: 然后关于这种检测我想说的最后一件事是，如果你不知道该做什么或所有其他条件相同，那么关注——

speaker: Unknown
en: ... because if you don't know what to do, or all else being equal, then focus on onboarding.
zh: ……因为如果你不知道该做什么，或所有其他条件相同，那么关注入职引导。

speaker: Unknown
en: Almost all companies have a whole lot more cancellation in the first day, 30 days, 90 days, depends, but the first period than the whole rest of the customer's life.
zh: 几乎所有公司在第一天、30 天、90 天内，视情况而定，但在第一时期的取消都比客户生命的其余部分多得多。

speaker: Unknown
en: And also, small changes in the onboarding can have large effects on cancellation, whereas later on, that's not necessarily true.
zh: 而且，入职引导的微小变化会对取消产生巨大影响，而在后期，这不一定成立。

speaker: Unknown
en: It could be, but it's not necessarily true.
zh: 可能是这样，但这未必是真的。

speaker: Unknown
en: So a really dramatic version of this is if you've ever done YouTube videos, which I know you have, but if a listener has ever done a YouTube video and you see the "retention", quote unquote, of the viewer on a YouTube video, it has this thing where it falls just so much, you can't believe, in the first 30 seconds, and then, if it's a decent video, it'll flatten out as people decide to watch the video.
zh: 所以这一个非常戏剧化的版本是，如果你曾经做过 YouTube 视频，我知道你做过，但如果听众曾经做过 YouTube 视频，并且你看到了观众在 YouTube 视频上的「留存率」，引用一下，它有一种情况，就是下降得非常多，你无法相信，在前 30 秒，然后，如果这是一个还不错的视频，它就会平稳下来，因为人们决定观看这个视频。

speaker: Unknown

speaker: Unknown
en: They raise prices, they're like, "I was scared," but then signups went up.
zh: 他们提高了价格，他们会说，“我很害怕，”但随后注册量却上升了。

speaker: Unknown
en: I once talked to a guy, this is really funny.
zh: 我曾经和一个人聊过，这真的很有趣。

speaker: Unknown
en: I'm going to not say the name to protect the name.
zh: 我不会说出名字以保护隐私。

speaker: Unknown
en: So he had a product that he was selling essentially to enterprise and government, so larger companies, and it was to me way too cheap.
zh: 所以他有一个产品，基本上是卖给企业和政府的，所以是大公司，而且对我来说太便宜了。

speaker: Unknown
en: So he said something like, "Yeah, I charge $300."
zh: 所以他说了类似这样的话，“是的，我收费 300 美元。”

speaker: Unknown
en: I'm like, "$300 a month, that's not enough."
zh: 我说，“300 美元一个月，这不够。”

speaker: Unknown
en: He goes, "No, per year."
zh: 他说，“不，一年。”

speaker: Unknown
en: They're like, "Okay, wait."
zh: 他们说，“好的，等一下。”

speaker: Unknown
en: I said, "Okay, how many signups do you get a week?"
zh: 我说，“好的，你一周能获得多少个注册？”

speaker: Unknown
en: And he's like, "One or two," because this is enterprise and it was a startup.
zh: 他说，“一两个”，因为这是企业客户，而且这是一家初创公司。

speaker: Unknown
en: I said, "Okay.
zh: 我说，“好的。

speaker: Unknown
en: Just for fun, just change it from per year to per month," So in other words, we're 12x-ing the price.
zh: 纯粹为了好玩，把它从每年改成每月，”换句话说，我们将价格提高 12 倍。

speaker: Unknown
en: So he did, and he still got one or two per week.
zh: 所以他照做了，而他仍然每周获得一两个。

speaker: Unknown
en: Nothing changed.
zh: 没有任何改变。

speaker: Unknown
en: I'm like, "Okay, what are you going to do next?"
zh: 我说，“好的，你接下来打算做什么？”

speaker: Unknown
en: And he goes, "Oh my gosh, well, now I have so much more money and profit, so I'm going to hire an engineer.
zh: 他说，“天哪，好吧，现在我有这么多更多的钱和利润，所以我打算雇一名工程师。

speaker: Unknown
en: I'm going to do this marketing."
zh: 我打算做这个营销。”

speaker: Unknown
en: And I'm like, "Time out.
zh: 我说，“暂停。

speaker: Unknown
en: What you're going to do is raise prices again.
zh: 你要做的是再次提价。

speaker: Unknown
en: You just told me you 12x-ed the price and nothing observable changed.
zh: 你刚才告诉我你把价格提高了 12 倍，而没有任何可观察到的变化。

speaker: Unknown
en: That means you're not near the price yet, right?
zh: 这意味着你还没有接近定价底线，对吧？

speaker: Unknown
en: You don't have to 10x it again necessarily.
zh: 你不必非要再提高 10 倍。

speaker: Unknown
en: Maybe 2x, maybe 50%, but you're not done.
zh: 也许 2 倍，也许 50%，但你还没完。

speaker: Unknown
en: You can do those other things too, but you're not done with the price."
zh: 你也可以做那些其他事情，但你还没有搞定价格。”

speaker: Unknown
en: It didn't even occur to him still.
zh: 他甚至还没想到这一点。

speaker: Unknown
en: Okay.
zh: 好的。

speaker: Unknown
en: So why does this happen?
zh: 那么为什么会发生这种情况？

speaker: Unknown
en: The reason is that pricing selects the market.
zh: 原因在于定价筛选了市场。

speaker: Unknown
en: So if you only think of the market as people with very limited budgets, barely can do anything, not getting much value out of it, then it is true that if you raise prices, you'll get fewer of them, because they were never getting that much value out of it anyway.
zh: 所以如果你只把市场看作是那些预算非常有限、几乎做不了任何事、从中获得不了多大价值的人，那么确实，如果你提价，你会得到更少这样的人，因为他们本来就没从中获得多大价值。

speaker: Unknown
en: They don't have that much money so if you raise prices, they're gone.
zh: 他们没有那么多钱，所以如果你提价，他们就走了。

speaker: Unknown
en: But think about just even a midsize company.
zh: 但哪怕只考虑一家中型公司呢。

speaker: Unknown
en: Forget about enterprise, just think about a company with a thousand employees and 400 million in revenue or whatever, and if they see a product that's $2 a month or even $100 a month, the thought is like, well, that can't be good enough.
zh: 别管企业级，就想一家拥有一千名员工、四亿美元营收或类似规模的公司，如果他们看到一个每月 2 美元甚至 100 美元的产品，想法就会是，好吧，这东西不够好。

speaker: Unknown
en: They're not mature enough, it's not going to do enough.
zh: 他们不够成熟，它做不了太多事。

speaker: Unknown
en: The support's not going to be good enough.
zh: 支持服务不会够好。

speaker: Unknown
en: They probably don't have good governance policies or other things that we need, et cetera.
zh: 他们可能没有良好的治理策略或我们需要的东西等等。

speaker: Unknown
en: Whether that's true or not, this is what it looks like because is it's low quality, cheap, whatever, aimed at SMB.
zh: 不管这是不是真的，这就是它看起来那样，因为它看起来是低质量、廉价，不管怎样，是针对 SMB 的。

speaker: Unknown
en: So they just won't buy, they're not in the market for the thing.
zh: 所以他们就是不会买，他们不是这个产品的市场。

speaker: Unknown
en: So it's not true that they have this demand curve where, oh, since it's cheap, they all want it.
zh: 所以并不是他们有那种需求曲线，哦，因为它便宜，他们都想要它。

speaker: Unknown
en: That's what microeconomics curve says.
zh: 那是微观经济学曲线说的。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's so cheap that they should all want it.
zh: 它太便宜了，他们应该都想要它。

speaker: Unknown
en: No, they don't.
zh: 不，他们不想要。

speaker: Unknown
en: None of them want it because it looks bad.
zh: 他们没人想要，因为它看起来很糟。

speaker: Unknown
en: So as it gets into a price range that makes sense for the kinds of things that they need, then their demand actually goes up.
zh: 所以当它进入一个对他们需要的那种东西有意义的价格区间时，他们的需求实际上会上升。

speaker: Unknown
en: Then it can stay up while it's in a good range, and then of course, at some point you are priced out of them.
zh: 然后当它处在一个好的区间时，它可以保持高位，当然，在某个点上，你会因价格过高而被他们排除。

speaker: Unknown
en: That particular kind of company's like, "Look, I'm not going to spend $10 million a year on it.
zh: 那种特定的公司会说，“看，我不会每年在上面花一千万美元。

speaker: Unknown
en: Are you kidding?"
zh: 你开什么玩笑？”

speaker: Unknown
en: So yes, it does slope down and go away.
zh: 所以是的，它确实会向下倾斜并消失。

speaker: Unknown
en: So it's not a normal curve, but it is like it slopes up and then it's something and slopes down.
zh: 所以它不是一条正常的曲线，但它就像它先向上倾斜，然后是某种东西，然后向下倾斜。

speaker: Unknown
en: Who knows exactly what shape it is?
zh: 谁确切知道它是什么形状？

speaker: Unknown
en: Probably none of us know, but it's more like a Mesa and not a line that goes up to down like in the textbook, for that market.
zh: 可能我们没人知道，但它更像一个平顶山，而不是教科书里那种从上到下的直线，对于那个市场而言。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's only the very lowest, you might even say worst in terms of metrics end of the market that has the microeconomic slope that you're worried about.
zh: 只有市场的最低端，你甚至可以说是在指标方面最差的那一端，才具有你担心的那种微观经济学斜率。

speaker: Unknown
en: So what happens is you raise prices and you enter a different market, and that's why the signups go up or okay.
zh: 所以发生的是你提高了价格，然后你进入了一个不同的市场，这就是为什么注册量会上升或者好吧。

speaker: Unknown
en: You leave behind perhaps a worse market anyway.
zh: 你甩掉的可能反正是一个更糟糕的市场。

speaker: Unknown
en: And of course, everyone will tell you the more they pay, the higher retention is, and all the kinds of stuff gets better when you charge more.
zh: 当然，每个人都会告诉你，他们付得越多，留存率就越高，当你收费更高时，所有这类事情都会变得更好。

speaker: Unknown
en: So this question, is pricing correct?
zh: 那么，这个问题，定价正确吗？

speaker: Unknown
en: This is what's in my mind when I ask that question.
zh: 这就是我问这个问题时脑子里的想法。

speaker: Unknown
en: Probably the answer is no because pricing's very hard.
zh: 答案可能是否定的，因为定价非常难。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's just as much art as it is science.
zh: 它既是艺术也是科学。

speaker: Unknown
en: You've had some really good people on here on pricing.
zh: 你这里请过一些非常擅长定价的人。

speaker: Unknown
en: In fact, so good that I've bought some of the books that those people have talked about because I loved the interview so much, so I believe in all that.
zh: 事实上，太好了以至于我都买了那些人谈到的一些书，因为我太喜欢那次采访了，所以我相信所有那些。

speaker: Unknown
en: No problem, I believe in it.
zh: 没问题，我相信它。

speaker: Unknown
en: Nevertheless, they also say it's art and science and it's very difficult to ... And also, once you auger it in, the world changes.
zh: 尽管如此，他们也说这是艺术和科学，而且很难……而且，一旦你把它定下来，世界就变了。

speaker: Unknown
en: Five, 10 years later, the market is different, the world's different, and so it's still unclear.
zh: 五到十年后，市场不同了，世界也不同了，所以仍然不清楚。

speaker: Unknown
en: Also, price is not just the number on the webpage.
zh: 此外，价格不仅仅是网页上的数字。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's easy to think that, but how it's structured is just as important, how the product's positioned is just as important.
zh: 这么想很容易，但它的结构同样重要，产品的定位同样重要。

speaker: Unknown
en: So for example, this example I've written about before online is this example, it actually was something that happened in my life but I changed the story to make it simple, and it's real unclear without having to get into lots of detail.
zh: 所以举个例子，这个我曾经在网上写过的例子实际上发生在我生活中但我改了故事让它变简单，而且如果不需深入很多细节的话真的不清楚。

speaker: Unknown
en: So the story version is how this company was able to charge eight times as much for the same product just by talking about it differently.
zh: 所以故事版本是这家公司如何能够通过用不同的方式谈论同一个产品而收取八倍的价格。

speaker: Unknown
en: So just by positioning it differently, eight times as much.
zh: 所以仅仅通过不同的定位，八倍的价格。

speaker: Unknown
en: Again, this happened to me but it's too complicated.
zh: 同样，这发生在我身上但这太复杂了。

speaker: Unknown
en: Those details are not interesting.
zh: 那些细节并不有趣。

speaker: Unknown
en: So say there's this company called Double Down, and the idea is that it halves the cost of your AdWords because it makes it so efficient, so that's what it says on the webpage.
zh: 所以说有这么一家叫 Double Down 的公司，想法是它将你的 AdWords 成本减半，因为它让它如此高效，这就是网页上写的。

speaker: Unknown
en: "Cut your AdWords cost in half," which is a very good pitch, isn't it?
zh: “把你的 AdWords 成本减半，”这是一个非常好的推销，不是吗？

speaker: Unknown
en: It's simple, obviously valuable.
zh: 它简单，明显有价值。

speaker: Unknown
en: But when you think ... So let's suppose I'm a customer and I spend $40,000 a month on AdWords.
zh: 但是当你思考……所以假设我是一个客户，我每个月在 AdWords 上花费四万美元。

speaker: Unknown
en: What am I willing to pay for double down?
zh: 我愿意付多少钱给 Double Down？

speaker: Unknown
en: Well, if you do cut my AdWords in half, then all right, I saved 20K, but I'm not willing to give 20K to Double Down because then I'm not saving any money.
zh: 好吧，如果你真的把我的 AdWords 减半了，那么好吧，我省了两万，但我并不愿意给 Double Down 两万，因为那样我就没省下任何钱了。

speaker: Unknown
en: In order to actually save money, I need to give Double Down less money.
zh: 为了真正省钱，我需要给 Double Down 更少的钱。

speaker: Unknown
en: How much less?
zh: 少多少？

speaker: Unknown
en: I don't know.
zh: 我不知道。

speaker: Unknown
en: Let's just call it a quarter.
zh: 我们就算四分之一吧。

speaker: Unknown
en: So I pay Double Down 5K to save 20, so I'm really saving 15, Double Down's making 5K a month, that's pretty good.
zh: 所以我付给 Double Down 5K 来省下 20，所以我其实省了 15，Double Down 一个月赚 5K，这很不错。

speaker: Unknown
en: Everyone's pretty happy at this five grand a month price point.
zh: 在这个 5000 美元一个月的价位上，大家都挺满意。

speaker: Unknown
en: So there's nothing wrong with this.
zh: 所以这没什么问题。

speaker: Unknown
en: No one's doing anything wrong, that's a perfectly valid company.
zh: 谁也没做错什么，这是一家完全合理的公司。

speaker: Unknown
en: However, think about these two situations that the CMO might be or the chief product officer might be in in talking to the CEO at the end of the year.
zh: 不过，想想 CMO 或首席产品官在年底向 CEO 汇报时可能面临的这两种情况。

speaker: Unknown
en: Well, scenario one goes, we started using this tool, Double Down, and it had our costs, so we're able to spend that money on some other stuff.
zh: 好的，情况一是这样的，我们开始使用这个工具，Double Down，它降低了我们的成本，所以我们能够把那笔钱花在其他东西上。

speaker: Unknown
en: We were able to save money."
zh: 我们能够省钱。"

speaker: Unknown
en: And the CEO would say, "Great, that's good.
zh: CEO 会说，“太好了，这很好。

speaker: Unknown
en: We're going to renew and I'm happy to hear it."
zh: 我们会续约，我很高兴听到这个。”

speaker: Unknown
en: Again, nothing wrong here, but let's take a different tact altogether.
zh: 再说一次，这里没问题，但我们完全换个角度试试。

speaker: Unknown
en: What does the CEO want to hear more?
zh: CEO 更想听什么？

speaker: Unknown
en: Growth or saves money?
zh: 增长还是省钱？

speaker: Unknown
en: Both are good, but I know which one is healthier for the company, increases market share, is better competitively, and also makes the company more valuable.
zh: 两者都好，但我知道哪一个对公司更健康，能增加市场份额，在竞争中更好，也能让公司更有价值。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's the growth.
zh: 是增长。

speaker: Unknown
en: So what we'd really like the CMO to tell the CEO is, "I increased the growth rate of the company."
zh: 所以我们真正希望 CMO 告诉 CEO 的是，“我提高了公司的增长率。”

speaker: Unknown
en: Not so much, "I save money."
zh: 而不是那么强调，“我省了钱。”

speaker: Unknown
en: That would be way better.
zh: 那样会好得多。

speaker: Unknown
en: So here's how we could do that with Double Down.
zh: 所以这就是我们可以用 Double Down 做到这一点的方式。

speaker: Unknown
en: Yes, Double Down halves the cost, but what that means is right now, right now, the company is paying 200 bucks per lead.
zh: 是的，Double Down 将成本减半，但这意味着现在，现在，公司为每个线索支付 200 美元。

speaker: Unknown
en: Let's call them leads, whatever this is outputting.
zh: 我们就叫它们线索吧，不管它输出的是什么。

speaker: Unknown
en: Well, if I halve the cost of a lead, I could get twice the leads for the same money.
zh: 好吧，如果我让线索的成本减半，我可以用同样的钱获得两倍的线索。

speaker: Unknown
en: I'm already willing to spend $200 a lead and I'm already spending 40K a month for it, so if Double Down halves the cost, it means I can get more leads.
zh: 我已经愿意花 200 美元一个线索，而且我已经每个月为此花费 40K，所以如果 Double Down 将成本减半，意味着我可以获得更多线索。

speaker: Unknown
en: So the way I could pitch Double Down is double the leads per month, period.
zh: 所以我可以推销 Double Down 的方式是每个月线索翻倍，句号。

speaker: Unknown
en: Now, if I'm willing to spend 40K for this number of leads, how much am I willing to spend to double the leads?
zh: 现在，如果我愿意花 40K 来获得这个数量的线索，那么我愿意花多少钱来让线索翻倍？

speaker: Unknown
en: I just said I'm willing to spend 40K for this number of leads, so doubling it, I'm willing to spend 40K to double it.
zh: 我刚说了我愿意花 40K 获得这个数量的线索，所以翻倍的话，我愿意花 40K 来翻倍。

speaker: Unknown
en: So if I give Double Down 40K, not five for the same product, which is the leads are cheaper, but now the pitch is I doubled the leads for 40K instead of having the cost for 5K.
zh: 所以如果我给 Double Down 40K，而不是同样产品的 5K，也就是线索更便宜了，但现在推销话术是我在 40K 的成本下让线索翻倍，而不是 5K 的成本。

speaker: Unknown
en: So Double Down gets 8x the money because it gets 40K for this product, not 5K for this product.
zh: 所以 Double Down 获得了 8 倍的钱，因为它因为这个产品拿到了 40K，而不是 5K。

speaker: Unknown
en: Amazing story.
zh: 惊人的故事。

speaker: Unknown
en: And everyone's happy because the CEO goes, "What did you do?"
zh: 大家都很开心，因为 CEO 说，“你做了什么？”

speaker: Unknown
en: And they said, "Oh my God, I doubled leads."
zh: 然后他们说，“天哪，我把线索翻倍了。”

speaker: Unknown
en: "What?"
zh: “什么？”

speaker: Unknown
en: "Yeah.
zh: “是的。

speaker: Unknown
en: At the same ROI as we had before, same CAC, I doubled leads."
zh: 在与我们之前相同的 ROI 下，相同的 CAC，我把线索翻倍了。”

speaker: Unknown
en: CEO goes, "How can we do more of that? "
zh: CEO 说，“我们要怎么做更多这样的事？”

speaker: Unknown
en: Just everything is so much better, same product.
zh: 一切都好太多了，同样的产品。

speaker: Unknown
en: Now, I know it's a little bit of an exaggeration, et cetera, because I'm trying to make a point, but the big point is, the largest point is pricing is not just the number on the page.
zh: 现在，我知道这有点夸张，等等，因为我试图说明一点，但重点是，最大的重点是定价不仅仅是页面上的那个数字。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's positioning, it's how their budgets work, it's how it's structured.
zh: 它是定位，它是他们的预算如何运作，它是它是如何构建的。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's per site or it's per usage or it's per seat or it's per ... All of this stuff is part of what pricing is.
zh: 它是按站点还是按使用量还是按座位还是按……所有这些都是定价的一部分。

speaker: Unknown
en: And often, even if it's the same price or the same product, depending on how that's structured, it either seems fair and good or it seems unfair and too expensive or whatever.
zh: 而且，通常，即使是同样的价格或同样的产品，取决于它是如何构建的，它要么看起来公平和良好，要么看起来不公平和太贵，或者其他什么。

speaker: Unknown
en: And so in particular with the positioning, the big lesson for product managers is sell more of what the company values like growth.
zh: 所以特别是在定位方面，给产品经理的最大教训是销售更多公司重视的东西，比如增长。

speaker: Unknown
en: It doesn't have to be growth.
zh: 不一定是增长。

speaker: Unknown
en: It could be something else, the retention for their customers, how competitive they are in the market.
zh: 可能是其他东西，他们客户的留存率，他们在市场上的竞争力。

speaker: Unknown
en: There's various things they could value.
zh: 有各种他们可能重视的东西。

speaker: Unknown
en: Growth is an obvious one.
zh: 增长是一个显而易见的。

speaker: Unknown
en: Sell them that they're going to get more of what they value as opposed to saving, cutting, ROI, saves time, saves money, more efficient.
zh: 向他们推销，他们将获得更多他们重视的东西，而不是省钱、削减、ROI、省时间、省钱、更高效。

speaker: Unknown
en: And again, there's nothing wrong with saving money saves time.
zh: 同样，省钱省时间没有任何问题。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's just that it caps this price and this value that they perceive that you do.
zh: 只不过它封顶了他们感知到的这个价格和你所做的价值。

speaker: Unknown
en: Whereas if you deliver more of the value that they already value, it's I don't want to say uncapped completely, but the cap is maybe an order of magnitude higher than saving.
zh: 而如果你交付更多他们已经看重的价值，我不想说完全没有上限，但这个上限可能比节省高出一个数量级。

speaker: Unknown
en: So again, it's valuable to save.
zh: 所以再说一次，节省是有价值的。

speaker: Unknown
en: They're not doing anything wrong.
zh: 他们没做错任何事。

speaker: Unknown
en: That's not how to talk about what it is, and therefore help set the price.
zh: 这不是谈论它是什么的方式，因此无法帮助设定价格。

speaker: Unknown
