speaker: Unknown
en: You add features, you have different tiers, you change the pricing in some way with usage or seats or something that kind of goes up automatically as they get more value out of it, so I don't think that's terribly interesting to double click into.
zh: 你增加功能，你有不同的等级，你以某种方式改变定价，基于 usage 或 seats 或某种随着他们从中获得更多价值而自动上涨的东西，所以我不认为深入 double click 这一点非常有趣。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's sort of obvious.
zh: 这很明显。

speaker: Unknown
en: I would say, "Oh look, it's tied into pricing because their behavior," but the main thing is you want it to where the customer themselves would agree when they pay more, that they are getting more value.
zh: 我会说，“哦看，它跟 pricing 挂钩，因为他们的行为，”但主要的是你要让它达到客户自己也会同意当他们付更多钱时，他们获得了更多价值。

speaker: Unknown
en: Hopefully they even think they're getting far more value than the price going up.
zh: 希望他们甚至认为他们获得的价值远超过价格上涨。

speaker: Unknown
en: I'm going to say this as if it's precise, which it's not, but they need to feel like if the price doubles, "Yeah, but I'm getting five times the value, so that's fine."
zh: 我要说得好像这很精确，其实不然，但他们需要感觉到如果价格翻倍，“是的，但我获得了五倍的价值，所以没关系。”

speaker: Unknown
en: That should be the feeling, whether they can measure it or not.
zh: 这应该是那种感觉，不管他们能不能衡量它。

speaker: Unknown
en: A good way to do that is to say, "Well, then you should be measuring whether they're getting value out of it."
zh: 做到这一点的一个好方法是说，“那么你应该衡量他们是否从中获得了价值。”

speaker: Unknown
en: Often we measure usage metrics and other kinds of metrics within our product because we can.
zh: 通常我们会因为我们能而在我们的产品内衡量使用指标和其他类型的指标，

speaker: Unknown
en: But actually, what's really important is to measure how does the customer value this?
zh: 但实际上，真正重要的是衡量客户是如何评价这个的？

speaker: Unknown
en: And we need to measure that so that we make that go up because if we make that go up, they'll be willing to pay in whatever structure.
zh: 我们需要衡量它，以便让我们让它上升，因为如果我们让它上升，他们将愿意以任何结构付费。

speaker: Unknown
en: And if that isn't going up, they won't be willing to pay.
zh: 如果那个没有上升，他们将不愿意付费。

speaker: Unknown
en: So even if we start making them, they'll leave.
zh: 所以即使我们开始让他们付费，他们也会离开。

speaker: Unknown
en: And we all know, we've all probably done it ourselves, we've all had products we love, but then as we scale, the price goes up faster than we feel the value is, and then we start looking for other products.
zh: 而且我们都知道，我们可能自己都做过，我们都有过我们喜爱的产品，但随着我们扩展，价格上涨的速度快于我们所感受到的价值，然后我们开始寻找其他产品。

speaker: Unknown
en: We've all experienced that.
zh: 我们都经历过那种情况。

speaker: Unknown
en: So that's what I'm saying.
zh: 所以这就是我想说的。

speaker: Unknown
en: To do that, you want some sort of measure of the value the customer's getting.
zh: 要做到这一点，你需要某种衡量客户所获价值的度量。

speaker: Unknown
en: If you're really lucky, that can be a number.
zh: 如果你真的很幸运，那可以是一个数字。

speaker: Unknown
en: That'd be wonderful.
zh: 那就太好了。

speaker: Unknown
en: Then go do that and maybe that's your North Star.
zh: 然后去做那个，也许那是你的北极星指标。

speaker: Unknown
en: But admittedly, it's not always possible.
zh: 但承认的是，这并不总是可能的。

speaker: Unknown
en: So then the question is, the usual questions and metrics, are there proxy metrics that we understand are not the full picture, but they're helpful, they're part of it?
zh: 那么问题就是，常见的问题和指标，有没有一些我们理解到不是全貌但很有助益、是其中一部分的代理指标？

speaker: Unknown
en: And I'm a big believer in saying not all important things are numbers.
zh: 我非常相信这一句：并非所有重要的东西都是数字。

speaker: Unknown
en: I mean, even things like how differentiated are we in the market?
zh: 我的意思是，甚至是像我们在市场上的差异化程度如何？这样的事情。

speaker: Unknown
en: Not a number, but it's very important.
zh: 不是数字，但它非常重要。

speaker: Unknown
en: So this might be one of those things that's important, but not a number.
zh: 所以这可能是属于那种重要但不是数字的事情之一。

speaker: Unknown
en: So okay, can we get some proxy metrics, even of behavior and other things that's something better than some metric that's just operational?
zh: 所以好吧，我们能得到一些代理指标吗，甚至是关于行为和其他事情的东西，比单纯的运营性指标更好。

speaker: Unknown
en: And even if it's qualitative, okay, can we do that?
zh: 即使它是定性的，好吧，我们能做吗？

speaker: Unknown
en: Can we talk to customers and ask them qualitative questions to try to see?
zh: 我们能和客户交谈并向他们询问定性问题以尝试观察吗？

speaker: Unknown
en: I would just say do your best here, because only when you generate more value for the customer, you can then decide how to split that with the customer in terms of things like price.
zh: 我只想说在这里尽力而为，因为只有当你为客户创造更多价值时，你才能决定如何在价格等方面与客户分割那个价值。

speaker: Unknown
en: But that's in fact how I think of it, that very phrase.
zh: 但实际上我就是这么想的，就是那个短语。

speaker: Unknown
en: How do we create more value for the customer and then split that with them?
zh: 我们如何为客户创造更多价值，然后与他们分割那个价值？

speaker: Unknown
en: And when you do that, you're keeping the customer forefront in mind.
zh: 当你这样做时，你把客户放在最重要的位置。

speaker: Unknown
en: You are taking some.
zh: 你拿走一些。

speaker: Unknown
en: Splitting means you get some.
zh: 分割意味着你得到一些。

speaker: Unknown
en: Let's not forget.
zh: 我们别忘记。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's not a charity.
zh: 这不是慈善。

speaker: Unknown
en: And on the other hand, first we should think, how do we generate value for customers, and then we've now earned the ability to take a little piece of that.
zh: 另一方面，首先我们应该思考，我们如何为客户创造价值，然后我们已经赢得了从中拿一小部分的能力。

speaker: Unknown
en: So to me, this is the right way to think about NRR, not just, "We'll add a feature and make them pay."
zh: 所以对我来说，这是思考 NRR 的正确方式，而不仅仅是，“我们将添加一个功能并让他们付费。”

speaker: Unknown
en: True, but let's actually take it from this different... Let's get there from this different perspective.
zh: 真的，但实际上让我们从这个不同的……让我们从这个不同的角度达到那里。

speaker: Unknown
en: Amazing.
zh: 太棒了。

speaker: Unknown
en: In the recent Modivon episode where we go into pricing, you actually have some really good tactical advice for measuring the value that you're giving to a company to quantify that, which feeds into this idea of how do I create more value for you and then how do we split it?
zh: 在最近我们讨论定价的 Modivon 剧集中，你实际上有一些非常好的战术性建议，用于衡量你给一家公司带来的价值以量化它，这归结到这个想法：我如何为你创造更多价值，然后我们如何分割它？

speaker: Jason Cohen
en: Yeah.
zh: 是的。

speaker: Unknown
en: The other element of this that's top of mind is just this land and expand strategy.
zh: 这其中另一个刻不容缓的要素就是这个落地并扩展策略。

speaker: Unknown
en: There's a lot of companies that are just like, "Okay, cool.
zh: 有很多公司就像，“好的，很酷。

speaker: Unknown
en: We'll get in with some price.
zh: 我们会以某个价格进入。

speaker: Unknown
en: We'll expand.
zh: 我们会扩展。

speaker: Unknown
en: That'll be amazing," which is essentially expanding as NRR going above 100%.
zh: 那将会是惊人的，”这本质上是扩展意味着 NRR 超过 100%。

speaker: Jason Cohen
en: Yes.
zh: 是的。

speaker: Unknown
en: Something Jen actually shared in her chat that was really important is that you can't expand that much, at least for a while, because if you get in for 10K, if you go up to, okay, now it's 100K, someone's going to be like, "What is this?
zh: Jen 实际上在她的分享中提到的一件非常重要的事情是，你不能扩展那么多，至少在一段时间内，因为如果你以 1 万进入，如果你涨到，好的，现在是 10 万，有人会像，“这是什么？

speaker: Unknown
en: This can't go up 10X.
zh: 这不可能涨 10 倍。

speaker: Unknown
en: Are we getting 10X value from this?"
zh: 我们从中获得了 10 倍的价值吗？”

speaker: Unknown
en: You can't just raise prices later.
zh: 你不能稍后只是提高价格。

speaker: Unknown
en: You're kind of stuck at that reference point, so you have to be really careful there.
zh: 你有点被卡在那个参考点，所以你必须在那里非常小心。

speaker: Unknown
en: Yeah, I think that's right.
zh: 是的，我认为那是正确的。

speaker: Unknown
en: And maybe you don't deserve it.
zh: 也许你不配得到它。

speaker: Unknown
en: In other words, especially when there's investors or other sort of forces saying like, "Hey, we need to X, Y, and Z," forces that are not the customer saying that, yeah, you can be coerced into making pricing or other kinds of policies that in fact are not good for the customer.
zh: 换句话说，特别是当有投资者或其他类型的力量像这样说，“嘿，我们需要 X、Y 和 Z，”这些不是客户说的力量，是的，你可能会被迫做出定价或其他实际上对客户不利的政策。

speaker: Unknown
en: So one tool I use is when anyone claims anything really, is, is that really true or is that really actually good for the customer?
zh: 我使用的一个工具是，当任何人声称任何事情时，那是真的吗，还是那实际上对客户真的有好处？

speaker: Unknown
en: Because look, we are going to do things that are selfishly good for us.
zh: 因为听着，我们要做那些对我们自身有利的自私的事。

speaker: Unknown
en: We have to.
zh: 我们必须这么做。

speaker: Unknown
en: We can't just do things that are bad for us, but is this in fact good for the customer?
zh: 我们不能做那些对我们不利的事，但这实际上对客户有利吗？

speaker: Unknown
en: Because often even in an internal proposal, we say it as if it is.
zh: 因为即使是在内部提案中，我们也经常把它说得好像有利一样。

speaker: Unknown
en: "Oh, this pricing will be good.
zh: “哦，这个定价会很不错。

speaker: Unknown
en: It'll raise prices on everyone, but it's better because of this reason," that we're sort of justifying.
zh: 它会提高每个人的价格，但因为这个原因会更好，”我们就是在进行这种辩解。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's like, well, will the customer say this is better?
zh: 就像是说，好吧，客户会觉得这更好吗？

speaker: Unknown
en: If the answer is no, it's like, okay, it's better for us, but sorry, we have an equation where it has to be better for us and better for the customer.
zh: 如果答案是否定的，那就是，好吧，这对我们更好，但抱歉，我们有一个公式，它必须对我们更好并且对客户也更好。

speaker: Unknown
en: Sorry, it's an and.
zh: 抱歉，这是一个“和”。

speaker: Unknown
en: And of course, not all companies do that.
zh: 当然，并非所有公司都这么做。

speaker: Unknown
en: And we experience that, all of us as consumers on the other end of that, and we don't like it.
zh: 我们都体验过这一点，作为终端消费者的我们，我们并不喜欢这样。

speaker: Unknown
en: And that's not a good long-term strategy, even though it might work in the short-term, as many bad long-term strategies are.
zh: 这不是一个好的长期战略，尽管它在短期内可能有效，就像许多糟糕的长期战略那样。

speaker: Unknown
en: I love just how this third step just reveals how powerful the sequence is that we're going through.
zh: 我喜欢这第三步只是揭示了我们要经历的这个流程是多么强大。

speaker: Unknown
en: Step one is this logo retention.
zh: 第一步是这个 logo 留存率。

speaker: Unknown
en: Essentially, do we have product market fit?
zh: 本质上，我们要有产品市场契合度吗？

speaker: Unknown
en: Step two is pricing, positioning.
zh: 第二步是定价、定位。

speaker: Unknown
en: Essentially, are we going after the right market and charging them the right amount roughly?
zh: 本质上，我们是否瞄准了正确的市场并大致向他们收取了正确的金额？

speaker: Unknown
en: And then here it's just, can we grow?
zh: 然后到这里就是，我们能增长吗？

speaker: Unknown
en: Is there something here that can continue to expand?
zh: 这里是否有某种可以持续扩张的东西？

speaker: Unknown
en: Because you're going to get eaten alive if you're, especially... And just to be clear, this is B2B SaaS primarily that we're talking about here.
zh: 因为你会被活生生吃掉，尤其是……为了明确起见，我们这里主要讨论的是 B2B SaaS。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's harder to grow NRR if you're a consumer product that has, I don't know, just a tier or two.
zh: 如果你是一个只有，我不知道，一两个层级的消费者产品，那提高 NRR 会更难。

speaker: Unknown
en: What I would say is the rules are true everywhere because they're based in the mechanics of finance in the business.
zh: 我要说的是，这些规则在任何地方都是真的，因为它们基于业务的金融机制。

speaker: Unknown
en: You're right that in the consumer segment or small business segment for that matter, they tend not to grow.
zh: 你是对的，在消费者领域或小企业领域，它们倾向于不增长。

speaker: Unknown
en: So that doesn't mean the NRR question is invalid.
zh: 所以这并不意味着 NRR 这个问题无效。

speaker: Unknown
en: It means, dang, we can't think of anything.
zh: 它的意思是，该死，我们想不出任何东西。

speaker: Unknown
en: That's okay.
zh: 那没关系。

speaker: Unknown
en: Then you go onto the next question because you can't think of anything, but it would be more strategic if we could.
zh: 然后你继续下一个问题，因为你想不出任何东西，但如果我们能想到，那会更具战略性。

speaker: Unknown
en: And are we trying hard enough?
zh: 以及我们是否足够努力？

speaker: Unknown
en: As a consumer, I do not want to spend more with AT&T, but they're also not giving me any more value.
zh: 作为一个消费者，我不想在 AT&T 上花更多钱，但他们也没有给我更多价值。

speaker: Unknown
en: But there are other products as a consumer, like Amazon, where they do.
zh: 但是作为消费者，还有其他产品，像亚马逊，他们确实给了。

speaker: Unknown
en: So you're right, but it would be as a product manager, it would be 10 times more valuable for you to think of something like that than to move on to other things, et cetera, or there's other ways, like other products.
zh: 所以你是对的，但作为一个产品经理，去想出那样的东西对你来说会比转移到其他事情上等等要有价值 10 倍，或者还有其他方式，比如其他产品。

speaker: Unknown
en: We didn't talk about it and it's okay because of course each one of these, you could go on forever, right?
zh: 我们没有讨论它，这没关系，因为当然这些步骤中的每一个，你都可以永远讨论下去，对吧？

speaker: Unknown
en: But another way is a second product.
zh: 但另一种方式是第二个产品。

speaker: Unknown
en: A second product sold to the same segments that you're in so that your existing customers can buy it.
zh: 向你所在的同一细分市场销售第二个产品，以便你的现有客户可以购买它。

speaker: Unknown
en: Well, I don't know why that wouldn't work in consumer.
zh: 嗯，我不知道为什么这在消费者领域行不通。

speaker: Unknown
en: It certainly works in consumer in apparel.
zh: 它在服装领域的消费者中当然行得通。

speaker: Unknown
en: I think about AG1, which has all these new... I'm doing their sleep supplement, and there it goes.
zh: 我想到了 AG1，它有所有这些新……我正在服用他们的睡眠补充剂，就是这样。

speaker: Unknown
en: They're just like, "Here's a new thing you can buy."
zh: 他们就像，“这是你可以买的新东西。”

speaker: Unknown
en: Yeah.
zh: 是的。

speaker: Unknown
en: So is it harder?
zh: 那么这更难吗？

speaker: Unknown
en: Yeah, of course, of course.
zh: 是的，当然，当然。

speaker: Unknown
en: All of these are easier, harder in different segments and ways.
zh: 所有这些在不同的细分领域和方式中都更容易或更难。

speaker: Unknown
en: Of course, of course.
zh: 当然，当然。

speaker: Unknown
en: I'm not trying to say otherwise.
zh: 我并不试图说不是这样。

speaker: Unknown
en: But I would say the mechanics of how the finances work is the same.
zh: 但我要说的是金融运作的机制是相同的。

speaker: Unknown
en: You're just saying, " I don't have this lever to pull."
zh: 你只是在说，“我没有这个杠杆可以拉。”

speaker: Unknown
en: And then I would say, "Okay, well then you need different levers."
zh: 然后我会说，“好吧，那么你需要不同的杠杆。”

speaker: Unknown
en: One that we didn't talk about is one way to offset the cancellations is existing customers grow, but another way is if existing customers bring in new customers.
zh: 我们没有谈论的其中一个方法是抵消取消的一种方式是现有客户的增长，但另一种方式是如果现有客户带来新客户。

speaker: Unknown
en: So they didn't grow, but they brought their friend.
zh: 所以他们没有增长，但他们带来了他们的朋友。

speaker: Unknown
en: Now, this is absolutely something that happens in consumer, but it's also an answer to this thing where cancellations grow exponentially, because existing customers bring in new does triple if you have more existing customers.
zh: 现在，这绝对是发生在消费者领域的事情，但这也是对取消量呈指数级增长的一个解决方案，因为如果现有客户带来新客户，当你有更多现有客户时，这一数字会变成三倍。

speaker: Unknown
en: Aha.
zh: Aha。

speaker: Unknown
en: So this is stuff like refer a friend and all this kind of stuff.
zh: 所以这就像是推荐朋友之类的东西。

speaker: Unknown
en: Again, some of this is obvious.
zh: 同样，其中有些是显而易见的。

speaker: Unknown
en: We don't need to enumerate that.
zh: 我们不需要一一列举。

speaker: Unknown
en: So those things are good.
zh: 所以这些事情是好的。

speaker: Unknown
en: And so in consumer, you might say, "Oh, it's easier to try to get someone to invite a friend with a coupon and blah, blah, blah, blah, than it is to try to get them to grow," but in B2B, that may not be true.
zh: 因此在消费者领域，你可能会说，“哦，试着用优惠券让别人邀请朋友比试图让他们增长要容易”，等等，等等，但在 B2B 中，这可能不成立。

speaker: Unknown
en: I don't get a mid-size company to refer.
zh: 我不会让一家中型公司去推荐。

speaker: Unknown
en: That doesn't make sense.
zh: 这说不通。

speaker: Unknown
en: So once again, this question of how do we have the existing base help us grow is still correct in consumer, but its manifestation could be very different.
zh: 所以再一次，关于我们如何让现有基础帮助我们增长这个问题在消费者领域仍然是正确的，但它的表现形式可能非常不同。

speaker: Unknown
en: Of course, I agree with that.
zh: 当然，我同意这一点。

speaker: Unknown
en: But I mean, how could we not say... I mean, of course, things like word of mouth and invite a friend, of course that's enormous with consumer, and this is one of the reasons why.
zh: 但我的意思是，我们怎么能不说……我的意思是，当然，像口碑和邀请朋友之类的事情，在消费者领域当然是巨大的，这也是原因之一。

speaker: Unknown
en: I just love picturing the people listening to this, especially product folks, founders.
zh: 我只是喜欢想象正在听这个节目的人，尤其是产品人员、创始人。

speaker: Unknown
en: I imagine many of them are just sitting here taking all these notes of how to help grow their product because we've positioned this conversation as how to deal with stalled growth, but it's actually just as useful, how do I grow more?
zh: 我想他们中的许多人正坐在这里记下关于如何帮助增长产品的所有笔记，因为我们把这次对话定位为如何处理增长停滞，但它同样有用，我如何增长得更多？

speaker: Unknown
en: Oh, for sure.
zh: 哦，当然。

speaker: Unknown
en: Right, right, right.
zh: 对，对，对。

speaker: Unknown
en: Which is awesome.
zh: 这很棒。

speaker: Unknown
en: It clearly is more growth.
zh: 这显然是更多的增长。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's just maybe a little more evocative, because if growth is good, yeah, sure, you want to grow more, but it's not the problem.
zh: 这可能只是更具启发性一点，因为如果增长是好的，是的，当然，你想增长更多，但这并不是问题。

speaker: Unknown
en: If growth is really good, the problem is generally operationally scaling to meet the growth, and so you're focused on that.
zh: 如果增长真的很好，问题通常在于运营上扩展以满足增长，所以你专注于那个。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's when growth slows, you're like, "Whoa, wait, wait, wait, wait.
zh: 当增长放缓时，你会想，“哇，等等，等等，等等，等等。

speaker: Unknown
en: We have to focus on growth now, must," as opposed to, "Of course it's always nice."
zh: 我们现在必须专注于增长，必须，”而不是说，“这当然总是好事。”

speaker: Unknown
en: So yeah.
zh: 所以是的。

speaker: Unknown
en: Yeah.

en: And it's attributed to Oscar Wilde, but I've tried to look into that as I tried to get all my annotations correct for the book.
zh: 这句话归于奥斯卡·王尔德名下，但我试图查证过，因为我试图为我书中的所有注释做正确修正。

speaker: Unknown
en: And there's no evidence that he said it.
zh: 并没有证据是他说的。

speaker: Unknown
en: But there's also no evidence who said it.
zh: 但也没有证据是谁说的。

speaker: Unknown
en: So let's say it's Oscar Wilde because he said lots of things like that.
zh: 所以我们就说是奥斯卡·王尔德吧，因为他说过很多那样的话。

speaker: Unknown
en: I love that.
zh: 我喜欢那个。

speaker: Unknown
en: And it's such a deep point.
zh: 这确实是个很深刻的观点。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's easy to hear and be like, "Yeah, yeah, yeah."
zh: 听起来很容易，你会像这样，“是啊，是啊，是啊。”

speaker: Unknown
en: But it's something I've learned to be more and more true over time, especially as you see people online doing their thing and just like, "Oh, I want to be like that."
zh: 但这是我在时间里学会变得越来越真实的事情，尤其是当你看到网上的人在干他们的事，你会想，“噢，我想像那样。”

speaker: Unknown
en: And then you realize, no, you got to be yourself.
zh: 然后你意识到，不，你得做你自己。

speaker: Unknown
en: No.
zh: 不。

speaker: Unknown
en: And the people who love you or like what you do also want you to be yourself because that's what they love.
zh: 那些爱你或喜欢你工作的人也希望你做你自己，因为那是他们爱的地方。

speaker: Unknown
en: And if you're changed, then they wouldn't love that.
zh: 如果你变了，那他们就不会爱那个了。

speaker: Unknown
en: Final question, you have this fancy award behind you on your desk.
zh: 最后一个问题，在你桌子后面的那个看起来很昂贵的奖。

speaker: Unknown
en: I am curious what's the story there.
zh: 我很好奇那里有什么故事。

speaker: Jason Cohen
en: That's the Ernst & Young Entrepreneur of the Year Award for-
zh: 那是安永年度企业家奖，颁发给——

speaker: Unknown
en: Whoo.
zh: 喔。

speaker: Unknown
en: ... 2017 for Central Texas, which I co-won with the CEO of WP Engine Heather Brunner, which is awesome because I often call Heather a late joining co-founder, because that's what ... LinkedIn, that's what Reid Hoffman called Jeff Wiener, because Jeff was four years in, but was so impactful to everything, the success of the company, the culture, the da, da, da, da, that basically is a co-founder.
zh: …… 2017 年的中德克萨斯奖，我和 WP Engine 的 CEO Heather Brunner 共同获得了这个奖，这很棒，因为我经常称 Heather 为迟来的联合创始人，因为那是…… LinkedIn，那是 Reid Hoffman 称呼 Jeff Wiener 的方式，因为 Jeff 进入了四年，但对所有事情都如此有影响力，公司的成功、文化、等等，等等，等等，等等，基本上就是联合创始人。

speaker: Unknown
en: And that's exactly what Heather is like at WP Engine.
zh: 而 Heather 在 WP Engine 就是那样。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's now been 11 years since she became the CEO.
zh: 距离她成为 CEO 已经 11 年了。

speaker: Unknown
en: So there's lots of data to back us up.
zh: 所以有很多数据支持我们。

speaker: Unknown
en: And I used to say to people at WP Engine, like, "If I just told you that Heather was a co-founder, you'd say, 'Yeah, no kidding.'"
zh: 我过去常对 WP Engine 的人说，比如，“如果我只是告诉你 Heather 是联合创始人，你会说，‘是啊，这有什么好奇怪的。’”

speaker: Unknown
en: I'm like, "Right, that's why I think of it that way because so do you, so did anyone, because that's the impact she's had."
zh: 我会说，“对，这就是我这么看待的原因，你也这么看，任何人都这么看，因为这就是她的影响力。”

speaker: Unknown
en: So we co-won that award, which is nice because you almost never have co-winners.
zh: 所以我们共同获得了那个奖，这很好，因为你几乎从来没有过共同获奖者。

speaker: Unknown
en: In fact, I can't remember another one.
zh: 事实上，我不记得有另一个了。

speaker: Unknown
en: I mean, I know there are others, but it's rare enough I can't think of another one.
zh: 我的意思是，我知道还有其他的，但这很罕见，我想不出另一个。

speaker: Unknown
en: So it's really cool that we co-won that entrepreneur award.
zh: 所以我们共同获得那个企业家奖真的很酷。

speaker: Unknown
en: Jason, this was so awesome.
zh: Jason，这太棒了。

speaker: Unknown
en: I really appreciate you making time.
zh: 非常感谢你抽出时间。

speaker: Unknown
en: I really appreciate you sharing so much wisdom with us.
zh: 非常感谢你与我们分享了这么多智慧。

en: Two final questions, where can folks find you online, point them to your book, your website, and how can listeners be useful to you?
zh: 最后两个问题，大家在哪里可以在线找到你，给他们指路你的书、你的网站，以及听众如何能对你有用？

speaker: Unknown
en: Yeah.

en: I mean, to be useful, order the book, hiddenmultipliers.com, or of course you don't have to.

en: I have all these articles online for free.
zh: 我在网上有所有这些免费文章。

speaker: Unknown
en: So you can go to asmartbear.com and I'm on Twitter and other stuff that's all linked off of that website.
zh: 所以你可以去 asmartbear.com，我在 Twitter 和其他东西上，都链接在那个网站上。

speaker: Unknown
en: And the articles, they're free.
zh: 还有文章，它们是免费的。

speaker: Unknown
en: I don't have ads, I don't sell courses, I don't sell anything.

speaker: Unknown
en: So that's very, very non-commercial.
zh: 所以这是非常、非常非商业化的。

speaker: Unknown
en: And so in fact, the one thing I've ever done with writing that costs money is the book because it's a physical book.
zh: 所以事实上，我在写作方面唯一一件花钱的事情就是书，因为它是一本实体书。

speaker: Unknown
en: I've got to charge something so I can ship it and everything.
zh: 我必须收点钱，这样我才能发货等等。

speaker: Unknown
en: But I think Hidden Multipliers is certainly my best work.
zh: 但我认为 Hidden Multipliers 当然是我最好的作品。

speaker: Unknown
en: So I'm very proud of that, but you don't have to buy it.
zh: 所以我为此感到非常自豪，但你不必买它。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's okay.
zh: 没关系。

speaker: Unknown
en: It'll make me feel good.
zh: 这会让我感觉很好。

speaker: Lenny
en: This is our chance to repay you for all the-
zh: 这是我们回报你所有机会——

speaker: Unknown
en: That's right.
zh: 没错。

speaker: Unknown
en: ... free content you've put out over time.
zh: ……你随着时间的推移发布的免费内容。

speaker: Jason Cohen
en: Right.

speaker: Unknown
en: And so I'm going to order a number of them.

en: Jason, thank you so much for being here.
